title: 2 Chronicles 1
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 Solomon son of David made his rule strong and safe. The LORD his God was with him and lifted him up very high.
2 Then Solomon spoke to all Israel. He spoke to the leaders of thousands and of hundreds, to the judges, and to every leader in all Israel — the heads of the families.
3 Solomon and all the people gathered went to the high place at Gibeon. God's Tent of Meeting was there. Moses the servant of the LORD had made it in the wilderness.
4 Now David had brought the ark of God from Kiriath-jearim to the place he had prepared for it. He had set up a tent for it in Jerusalem.
5 But the bronze altar made by Bezalel son of Uri, the son of Hur, was in Gibeon. It stood before the tabernacle of the LORD. So Solomon and the people gathered asked the LORD there.
6 Solomon offered sacrifices there before the LORD on the bronze altar in the Tent of Meeting. He offered a thousand burnt offerings.
7 That night God appeared to Solomon and said, "Ask, and I will give it to you!"
8 Solomon replied to God: "You have shown great faithful love to my father David. You have made me king in his place.
9 Now, O LORD God, make Your promise to my father David come true. You have made me king over a people as many as the dust on the earth.
10 Now give me wisdom and knowledge, so that I may lead this people. For who is able to rule this great people of Yours?"

11 God said to Solomon, "This was in your heart. You did not ask for riches or wealth or glory. You did not ask for the death of your enemies. You did not even ask for a long life. Instead you asked for wisdom and knowledge to lead My people, the people I have made you king over.
12 So wisdom and knowledge are given to you. I will also give you riches and wealth and honor. No king before you had this much, and no king after you will have this much."
13 So Solomon went to Jerusalem from the high place in Gibeon, from before the Tent of Meeting. And he ruled over Israel.
14 Solomon gathered 1,400 chariots and 12,000 horses. He kept them in the chariot cities and also with him in Jerusalem.
15 The king made silver and gold as common in Jerusalem as stones. He made cedar wood as plentiful as sycamore trees in the foothills.
16 Solomon's horses were brought in from Egypt and Kue. The king's traders bought them from Kue.
17 A chariot was brought in from Egypt for six hundred shekels of silver. A horse cost a hundred and fifty. In the same way, they sold them to all the kings of the Hittites and to the kings of Aram.

title: 2 Chronicles 2
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 Solomon decided to build a house for the Name of the LORD. He also planned a royal palace for himself.
2 So he chose 70,000 carriers, 80,000 stone workers in the mountains, and 3,600 foremen to watch the work.
3 Then Solomon sent a message to Hiram, the king of Tyre. He said, "Help me as you helped my father David. You sent him cedar wood to build his house."

4 "Look, I am going to build a house for the Name of the LORD my God. I will set it apart for Him. We will burn sweet-smelling incense there. We will keep the special bread on the table all the time. We will offer burnt offerings every morning and evening. We will offer them on the Sabbaths, the New Moons, and the chosen feasts of the LORD our God. This is God's lasting rule for Israel."
5 "The house I am building will be great. Our God is greater than all gods."
6 "But who can build a house for Him? The heavens, even the highest heavens, cannot hold Him. Who am I to build a house for Him? I can only make a place to burn sacrifices before Him."

7 "So send me a skilled worker. He must know how to work with gold, silver, bronze, and iron. He must also work with purple, deep red, and blue yarn. He will work with my workers in Judah and Jerusalem, whom my father David gave to me."
8 "Also send me cedar, cypress, and algum logs from Lebanon. I know your servants are good at cutting wood there. My servants will work with yours."
9 "They will prepare a lot of wood for me. The temple I am building will be great and wonderful."
10 "I will pay your servants, the woodcutters. I will give 20,000 cors of ground wheat, 20,000 cors of barley, 20,000 baths of wine, and 20,000 baths of olive oil."
11 Then Hiram, the king of Tyre, wrote back to Solomon. He said, "The LORD loves His people. That is why He made you their king."

12 Hiram also said, "Blessed be the LORD, the God of Israel, who made the heavens and the earth! He has given King David a wise son. This son has insight and understanding. He will build a temple for the LORD and a royal palace for himself."
13 "Now I am sending you Huram-abi. He is a skilled man, full of skill and ideas."

14 "His mother was from the daughters of Dan. His father was a man of Tyre. He is skilled in work with gold, silver, bronze, iron, stone, and wood. He works with purple, blue, and deep red yarn, and with fine cloth. He can carve any design. He can make any design that is given to him. He will work with your workers and with those of my lord, your father David."
15 "Now let my lord send the wheat, barley, olive oil, and wine to his servants, just as you promised."
16 "We will cut as many logs from Lebanon as you need. We will tie them as rafts and float them by sea to Joppa. Then you can carry them up to Jerusalem."
17 Solomon counted all the men in Israel who were not Israelites. He did this after his father David's count of the people. He found 153,600 of them.
18 Solomon made 70,000 of them carriers. He made 80,000 stone workers in the mountains. He made 3,600 foremen to watch the people at work.

title: 2 Chronicles 3
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 Then Solomon began to build the house of the LORD in Jerusalem on Mount Moriah. There the LORD had appeared to his father David. This was the place David had prepared on the threshing floor of Ornan the Jebusite.
2 Solomon began the work on the second day of the second month. It was the fourth year of his reign.
3 The foundation Solomon laid for the house of God was sixty cubits long and twenty cubits wide. He used the old standard.
4 The front porch ran across the width of the temple. It was twenty cubits long and twenty cubits high. He covered the inside with pure gold.
5 He lined the main room with cypress wood. Then he covered it with fine gold. He decorated it with palm trees and chains.
6 He set precious stones in the temple for beauty. The gold came from Parvaim.
7 He covered the beams, the door frames, the walls, and the doors with gold. He carved cherubim on the walls.
8 Then he made the Most Holy Place. Its length matched the width of the temple. It was twenty cubits long and twenty cubits wide. He covered the inside with six hundred talents of fine gold.
9 The nails weighed fifty shekels of gold. He also covered the upper area with gold.
10 In the Most Holy Place he made two cherubim of carved work. He covered them with gold.

11 The total wingspan of the cherubim was twenty cubits. One wing of the first cherub was five cubits long and touched the wall. Its other wing was five cubits long and touched the wing of the other cherub.
12 The wing of the second cherub also measured five cubits and touched the wall. Its other wing measured five cubits and touched the wing of the first cherub.
13 So the total wingspan of these cherubim was twenty cubits. They stood on their feet and faced the main room.
14 He made the veil of blue, purple, and deep red thread and fine linen cloth. He had cherubim woven into it.
15 In front of the temple he made two pillars. Together they were thirty-five cubits high. Each had a top piece of five cubits.
16 He made chains that were woven together. He put them on top of the pillars. He made one hundred pomegranates and fastened them to the chains.
17 Then he set up the pillars in front of the temple, one on the south and one on the north. He named the south pillar Jachin. He named the north pillar Boaz.

title: 2 Chronicles 4
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 He made a bronze altar. It was twenty cubits long, twenty cubits wide, and ten cubits high.
2 He also made the Sea of cast metal. It was round in shape. It was ten cubits from rim to rim and five cubits high. It was thirty cubits the way around.
3 Below the rim, oxen shapes went all around it. There were ten per cubit all around the Sea. They were cast in two rows as a part of the Sea.
4 The Sea stood on twelve oxen. Three faced north, three faced west, three faced south, and three faced east. The Sea rested on them. All their back parts pointed toward the center.
5 It was as thick as a hand's width. Its rim was shaped like the top of a cup, like a lily flower. It could hold three thousand baths.
6 He also made ten bowls for washing. He placed five on the south side and five on the north. The parts of the burnt offering were washed in them. But the priests used the Sea for washing.
7 He made ten gold lampstands as the plans said. He placed them in the temple, five on the south side and five on the north.
8 He also made ten tables. He placed them in the temple, five on the south side and five on the north. He also made a hundred gold bowls.
9 He made the court of the priests and the large court with its doors. He covered the doors with bronze.
10 He put the Sea on the south side, at the southeast corner.
11 Huram also made the pots, shovels, and sprinkling bowls. So Huram finished the work that he had started for King Solomon in the house of God:
12 the two pillars; the two bowl-shaped tops on the pillars; the two net-like coverings on both bowls of the tops on the pillars;
13 the four hundred pomegranates for the two net-like coverings (two rows of pomegranates for each net covering both bowl-shaped tops on the pillars);
14 the stands; the bowls on the stands;
15 the Sea; the twelve oxen under the Sea;
16 and the pots, shovels, meat forks, and all the other items. All these things that Huram-abi made for King Solomon for the house of the LORD were of shiny bronze.
17 The king had them shaped in clay molds. This was done in the plain of the Jordan between Succoth and Zeredah.
18 Solomon made all these items in such great numbers. The weight of the bronze could not be measured.
19 Solomon also made all the items for the house of God: the golden altar; the tables on which the Bread of the Presence was placed;
20 the lampstands of pure gold and their lamps. They burned in front of the inner room, as the rule said;
21 the flowers, lamps, and tongs of gold—of purest gold;
22 the wick trimmers, sprinkling bowls, spoons, and incense pans of purest gold; and the gold doors of the temple: the inner doors to the Most Holy Place and the doors of the main hall.

title: 2 Chronicles 5
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 So all the work Solomon did for the house of the LORD was finished. Then Solomon brought in the things his father David had set apart for God—the silver, the gold, and all the objects. He placed them in the storage rooms of the house of God.

2 Then Solomon called together in Jerusalem the elders of Israel. He called all the tribal heads and family leaders of the Israelites. They came to bring up the ark of the covenant of the LORD from Zion, the City of David.
3 So all the men of Israel came to the king at the feast in the seventh month.
4 When all the elders of Israel had arrived, the Levites picked up the ark.
5 They brought up the ark and the Tent of Meeting with all its holy objects. The Levitical priests carried them up.
6 There, in front of the ark, King Solomon and all the people of Israel met with him. They offered so many sheep and oxen as sacrifice that no one could count them.
7 Then the priests brought the ark of the covenant of the LORD to its place. They put it in the inner room of the temple, the Most Holy Place, under the wings of the cherubim.
8 The cherubim spread their wings over the place of the ark. They covered the ark and its poles.
9 The poles of the ark were long. Their ends could be seen from in front of the inner room, but not from outside the Holy Place. They are still there today.
10 There was nothing in the ark except the two stone tablets. Moses had placed them in it at Horeb. There the LORD had made a covenant with the Israelites after they came out of Egypt.
11 Now all the priests who were there had made themselves holy. This was true for every group of priests. Then the priests came out of the Holy Place.

12 All the Levitical singers stood on the east side of the altar. These were Asaph, Heman, Jeduthun, and their sons and relatives. They wore fine white cloth and played cymbals, harps, and lyres. With them stood 120 priests blowing trumpets.

13 The trumpet players and singers joined together. With one voice they praised and thanked the LORD. They lifted their voices with trumpets, cymbals, and music. They sang praise to the LORD: “For He is good; His loving devotion endures forever.” Then the temple, the house of the LORD, was filled with a cloud.
14 Because of the cloud, the priests could not stand there to serve. The glory of the LORD filled the house of God.

title: 2 Chronicles 6
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 Then Solomon said: "The LORD has said He would live in the dark cloud.
2 I have built You a great house, a place where You can live forever."
3 All the people of Israel stood there. The king turned around and blessed them all.
4 He said: "Blessed be the LORD, the God of Israel. He spoke with His mouth to my father David, and He has done it with His own hand. He said,
5 'From the day I brought My people out of Egypt, I did not choose a city from any tribe of Israel for a house for My Name. I did not choose any man to rule My people Israel.
6 But now I have chosen Jerusalem for My Name to be there. And I have chosen David to lead My people Israel.'
7 My father David wanted in his heart to build a house for the Name of the LORD, the God of Israel.
8 But the LORD said to my father David, 'You wanted in your heart to build a house for My Name. You did well to have this in your heart.
9 But you are not the one to build it. Your son, your own son, will build the house for My Name.'
10 Now the LORD has done what He said. I have followed my father David. I sit on the throne of Israel, as the LORD promised. I have built the house for the Name of the LORD, the God of Israel.
11 And there I have put a place for the ark. The ark holds the covenant the LORD made with the children of Israel."
12 Then Solomon stood before the altar of the LORD in front of all the people of Israel. He spread out his hands.

13 Solomon had made a bronze platform. It was five cubits long, five cubits wide, and three cubits high. He placed it in the middle of the courtyard. He stood on it, then knelt down in front of all the people. He spread out his hands toward heaven.
14 He said: "O LORD, God of Israel, there is no God like You in heaven or on earth. You keep Your covenant of faithful love with Your servants who walk before You with all their hearts.
15 You have kept Your promise to Your servant, my father David. What You spoke with Your mouth, You have done with Your hand today.

16 Now, O LORD, God of Israel, keep Your promise to Your servant, my father David. You said: 'You will always have a man to sit before Me on the throne of Israel, if your children guard their way and walk in My law, as you have walked before Me.'
17 And now, O LORD, God of Israel, please confirm what You promised to Your servant David.
18 But will God really live with man on the earth? Even heaven, the highest heaven, cannot hold You. How much less this temple I have built!
19 Yet hear the prayer and request of Your servant, O LORD my God. Hear the cry and the prayer that Your servant is praying before You.
20 May Your eyes be open toward this temple day and night. This is the place where You said You would put Your Name. Hear the prayer Your servant prays toward this place.
21 Hear the request of Your servant and of Your people Israel when they pray toward this place. Hear from heaven, Your home. Hear and forgive.
22 When a man sins against his neighbor and must make a promise before God, and he comes to make that promise before Your altar in this temple,
23 then hear from heaven and act. Judge Your servants. Punish the guilty man by bringing his own deeds back on his head. Declare the good man right and reward him for his right ways.
24 When Your people Israel are beaten by an enemy because they have sinned against You, and they return to You and confess Your name, praying and pleading before You in this temple,
25 then hear from heaven and forgive the sin of Your people Israel. Bring them back to the land You gave to them and their fathers.

26 When the sky is shut and there is no rain because Your people have sinned against You, and they pray toward this place and confess Your name, and they turn from their sins because You have brought trouble on them,
27 then hear from heaven. Forgive the sin of Your servants, Your people Israel. Teach them the good way they should walk. Send rain on the land You gave Your people as a gift.
28 When famine or sickness comes on the land, or crop disease or mold or locusts or grasshoppers, or when enemies surround them in war in their cities—any sickness or trouble that comes—
29 then hear from heaven, Your home, whatever prayer or request Your people Israel make. Each one knows his own trouble and spreads out his hands toward this temple.
30 Forgive each man and repay him for all his ways. You know his heart, for You alone know the hearts of all people.
31 Then they will fear You and walk in Your ways all the days they live in the land You gave to our fathers.
32 Even the foreigner, who is not from Your people Israel but has come from a far land because of Your great name and Your strong hand and reaching arm—when he comes and prays toward this temple,

33 then hear from heaven, Your home, and do all that the foreigner asks of You. Then all the peoples of the earth will know Your name and fear You, as Your people Israel do. They will know that this house I have built is called by Your Name.
34 When Your people go to war against their enemies, wherever You send them, and they pray to You facing the city You have chosen and the house I have built for Your Name,
35 then hear from heaven their prayer and their request, and help them win their case.
36 When they sin against You—for there is no one who does not sin—and You become angry with them and give them to an enemy who carries them away to a land far or near,

37 and when they think clearly again in the land where they were taken, and they turn back and beg You in the land of their captors, saying, 'We have sinned. We have done wrong. We have acted in evil ways,'

38 and when they return to You with all their heart and soul in the land of the enemies who took them, and they pray facing the land You gave their fathers, the city You have chosen, and the house I have built for Your Name,
39 then hear from heaven, Your home, their prayer and their request, and help their cause. Forgive Your people who sinned against You.
40 Now, my God, may Your eyes be open and Your ears listen to the prayer offered in this place.

41 Now rise up, O LORD God, and come to Your resting place, You and the ark of Your strength. May Your priests, O LORD God, be clothed with salvation, and may Your faithful people rejoice in goodness.
42 O LORD God, do not turn away Your chosen king. Remember Your faithful love to Your servant David."

title: 2 Chronicles 7
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 When Solomon finished praying, fire came down from heaven. It burned up the burnt offering and the sacrifices. And the glory of the LORD filled the temple.
2 The priests could not enter the house of the LORD, because the glory of the LORD filled it.

3 All the Israelites saw the fire come down and the glory of the LORD above the temple. They bowed down with their faces to the ground. They worshiped and thanked the LORD: "For He is good; His faithful love lasts forever."
4 Then the king and all the people offered sacrifices before the LORD.
5 King Solomon offered a sacrifice of 22,000 oxen and 120,000 sheep. So the king and all the people set apart the house of God as holy.

6 The priests stood at their places. The Levites stood with the musical instruments of the LORD. King David had made these to thank the LORD. With them David had given praise, saying, "His faithful love lasts forever." Across from the Levites, the priests blew trumpets. All the Israelites were standing.

7 Then Solomon set apart as holy the middle of the courtyard in front of the house of the LORD. There he offered the burnt offerings and the fat of the peace offerings. He did this because the bronze altar he had made could not hold all these offerings.
8 At that time Solomon and all Israel kept the feast for seven days. It was a very large group of people, from Lebo-hamath to the Brook of Egypt.
9 On the eighth day they held a special meeting. The dedication of the altar had lasted seven days, and the feast seven more days.
10 On the twenty-third day of the seventh month, Solomon sent the people home. Their hearts were full of joy for the good things the LORD had done for David, for Solomon, and for His people Israel.
11 Solomon finished the house of the LORD and the royal palace. He carried out all that was in his heart to do for the house of the LORD and his own palace.
12 Then the LORD appeared to him at night and said: "I have heard your prayer. I have chosen this place for Myself as a house of sacrifice.
13 If I close the sky so there is no rain, or if I send locusts to eat up the land, or if I send a plague among My people,

14 **If my people, who are called by my name, will humble themselves and pray and seek my face and turn from their wicked ways, then I will hear from heaven, and I will forgive their sin and will heal their land**.
15 Now My eyes will be open and My ears will listen to the prayers offered in this place.
16 I have chosen this temple and set it apart as holy, so that My Name may be there forever. My eyes and My heart will be there always.
17 And you, if you walk before Me as your father David walked, and if you do all I have commanded you, and if you keep My rules and laws,
18 then I will make your royal throne strong. I promised this to your father David when I said, 'You will always have a man to rule over Israel.'
19 But if you turn away and leave behind the rules and commands I have given you, and if you go to serve and worship other gods,

20 then I will pull Israel out of the land I have given them. And I will send away from My presence this temple I have set apart for My Name. I will make it something that all the peoples mock and laugh at.
21 This temple will become a pile of broken stones. All who pass by will be shocked. They will say, 'Why has the LORD done such a thing to this land and to this temple?'

22 Others will answer, 'Because they left the LORD, the God of their fathers, who brought them out of the land of Egypt. They have turned to other gods, worshiping and serving them. That is why He has brought all this trouble on them.'"

title: 2 Chronicles 8
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 Solomon worked for twenty years. In that time he built the house of the LORD and his own palace.
2 After this, Solomon rebuilt the cities Hiram had given to him. He sent Israelites to live in them.
3 Then Solomon went to Hamath-zobah and took it by war.
4 He built Tadmor in the desert. He also built all the storage cities in Hamath.
5 He rebuilt Upper Beth-horon and Lower Beth-horon. He made them strong walled cities with gates and iron bars.
6 He also built Baalath and all his storage cities. He built cities for his chariots and his horses. He built whatever he wanted in Jerusalem, in Lebanon, and in all the land under his rule.
7 Some people in the land were not Israelites. They were Hittites, Amorites, Perizzites, Hivites, and Jebusites.
8 Their children's children still lived in the land. The Israelites had not been able to destroy them. Solomon forced these people to work for him, and they still do this today.
9 But Solomon did not force any Israelite to work as a slave. The Israelites were his soldiers, his officers, his captains, and the leaders of his chariots and horse soldiers.
10 They were also the chief leaders for King Solomon. There were 250 leaders over the work.

11 Solomon brought Pharaoh's daughter up from the City of David. He took her to the palace he had built for her. He said, "My wife must not live in the house of David, the king of Israel. The places where the ark of the LORD has come are holy."
12 At that time Solomon gave burnt offerings to the LORD. He gave them on the altar of the LORD that he had built in front of the porch of the temple.

13 He gave the daily offerings as Moses had commanded. He did this for the Sabbaths, the New Moons, and the three yearly feasts. These were the Feast of Unleavened Bread, the Feast of Weeks, and the Feast of Tabernacles.

14 Solomon followed the rules of his father David. He put the groups of the priests in charge of their work. He put the Levites in charge of their duties. They led praise and helped the priests each day. He also put gate guards at each gate by their groups. David, the man of God, had commanded this.
15 They did not turn away from the king's command. They obeyed in matters about the priests, the Levites, and the rooms that held the wealth.
16 So all the work of Solomon was done well. It went from the day the base of the house of the LORD was laid until the work was finished. So the house of the LORD was completed.
17 Then Solomon went to Ezion-geber and to Eloth on the coast of Edom.

18 Hiram sent him ships led by his own men. He also sent groups of skilled sailors. They went with Solomon's servants to Ophir. From there they brought back 450 talents of gold and gave it to King Solomon.

title: 2 Chronicles 9
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 The queen of Sheba heard how great Solomon was. So she came to test him with hard questions. She came to Jerusalem with a very large group of travelers. Her camels carried spices, much gold, and precious stones. She came to Solomon and spoke about all that was on her mind.
2 Solomon answered all her questions. Nothing was too hard for him to explain.
3 The queen of Sheba saw the wisdom of Solomon. She also saw the palace he had built.

4 She saw the food on his table. She saw where his servants sat. She saw the work and the clothes of his helpers and the servants who poured his drinks. She also saw the burnt offerings he gave at the house of the LORD. She was amazed.
5 She said to the king, “The report I heard in my own country about your words and your wisdom is true.
6 But I did not believe the reports until I came and saw with my own eyes. Indeed, not even half of your great wisdom was told to me. You are far greater than the report I heard.
7 How blessed are your men! How blessed are these servants of yours who stand before you each day and hear your wisdom!

8 Blessed be the LORD your God, who was pleased with you. He set you on His throne to be king for the LORD your God. Because your God loved Israel and wanted them to last forever, He made you king over them. So you can do justice and righteousness.”
9 Then she gave the king 120 talents of gold, many spices, and precious stones. There had never been such spices as those the queen of Sheba gave to King Solomon.
10 (The servants of Hiram and the servants of Solomon brought gold from Ophir. They also brought algum wood and precious stones.

11 The king made the algum wood into steps for the house of the LORD and for the king’s palace. He also made lyres and harps for the singers. Nothing like this had been seen before in the land of Judah.)
12 King Solomon gave the queen of Sheba all she wanted. He gave her whatever she asked. He gave her far more than she had brought him. Then she went back to her own country with her servants.
13 Each year Solomon received 666 talents of gold.
14 This did not include the money from the traders and merchants. All the kings of Arabia and the rulers of the land also brought gold and silver to Solomon.
15 King Solomon made two hundred large shields of gold beaten flat. He used six hundred shekels of gold for each shield.
16 He also made three hundred small shields of gold beaten flat. He used three hundred shekels of gold for each shield. The king put them in the House of the Forest of Lebanon.
17 The king also made a great throne of ivory. He covered it with pure gold.
18 The throne had six steps. A gold stool for the feet was attached to it. There were armrests on both sides of the seat. A lion stood beside each armrest.
19 Twelve lions stood on the six steps. There was one lion at each end of each step. Nothing like this had ever been made for any kingdom.
20 All King Solomon’s drinking cups were gold. All the dishes in the House of the Forest of Lebanon were pure gold. There was no silver. Silver was counted as nothing in the days of Solomon.
21 The king had the ships of Tarshish. They sailed with Hiram’s servants. Once every three years the ships of Tarshish came back. They brought gold, silver, ivory, apes, and peacocks.
22 So King Solomon was greater than all the kings of the earth in riches and wisdom.
23 All the kings of the earth wanted to meet Solomon. They came to hear the wisdom that God had put in his heart.
24 Year after year, each visitor brought his gift to the king. They brought things of silver and gold, clothes, weapons, spices, horses, and mules.
25 Solomon had 4,000 small rooms for horses and chariots. He had 12,000 horses. He kept them in the chariot cities and also with him in Jerusalem.
26 He ruled over all the kings from the Euphrates River to the land of the Philistines. He ruled as far as the border of Egypt.
27 The king made silver as common as stones in Jerusalem. He made cedar wood as plentiful as sycamore trees in the low hills.
28 Solomon’s horses were brought from Egypt and from all the lands.

29 The rest of the acts of Solomon, from beginning to end, are written down. They are in the Records of Nathan the Prophet. They are in the Prophecy of Ahijah the Shilonite. They are in the Visions of Iddo the Seer about Jeroboam son of Nebat.
30 Solomon ruled in Jerusalem over all Israel forty years.
31 Then Solomon died and joined his fathers. He was buried in the city of his father David. His son Rehoboam ruled in his place.

title: 2 Chronicles 10
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 Then Rehoboam went to Shechem. All Israel had gone there to make him king.
2 Jeroboam son of Nebat heard about this. He came back from Egypt, where he had run from King Solomon.
3 So they sent for Jeroboam. He and all Israel came to Rehoboam and said,
4 “Your father put a heavy yoke on us. Now please make the load lighter. Then we will serve you.”
5 Rehoboam answered, “Come back to me in three days.” So the people left.
6 King Rehoboam talked with the older men (elders) who had served his father Solomon. He asked, “How should I answer these people?”
7 They said, “Be kind to these people. Speak kind words to them. Then they will serve you forever.”
8 But Rehoboam did not take the advice of the elders. He talked with the young men who had grown up with him and served him.
9 He asked them, “What should we say to these people? They told me, ‘Make the yoke your father put on us lighter.’”

10 The young men who had grown up with him said, “Answer the people like this. They said, ‘Your father made our yoke heavy. Now make it lighter.’ Tell them, ‘My little finger is thicker than my father’s waist!
11 My father put a heavy yoke on you. I will make it heavier. My father beat you with whips. I will beat you with whips full of sharp points.’”
12 After three days, Jeroboam and all the people came back to Rehoboam. The king had said, “Come back to me on the third day.”
13 The king answered them in a hard way. King Rehoboam did not take the advice of the elders.
14 He spoke to them as the young men had said. He told them, “My father made your yoke heavy. I will make it heavier. My father beat you with whips. I will beat you with whips full of sharp points.”
15 So the king did not listen to the people. This thing happened because of God. The LORD wanted to make true the word He spoke through Ahijah the Shilonite to Jeroboam son of Nebat.

16 All Israel saw that the king would not listen to them. They answered the king: “What share do we have in David? What part do we have in the son of Jesse? Go home to your tents, Israel! Look after your own house now, David!” So all the Israelites went home.
17 But Rehoboam still ruled over the Israelites who lived in the cities of Judah.

18 Then King Rehoboam sent out Hadoram. Hadoram was in charge of the hard work the king made people do. But the Israelites threw stones at him until he died. King Rehoboam quickly got into his chariot and ran to Jerusalem.
19 So Israel has turned against the house of David until this day.

title: 2 Chronicles 11
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 Rehoboam came to Jerusalem. He gathered the people of Judah and Benjamin. He had 180,000 trained soldiers. He wanted to fight Israel and take back the kingdom.
2 But the LORD spoke to Shemaiah the man of God. He said,
3 “Speak to Rehoboam son of Solomon, king of Judah. Also speak to all the Israelites in Judah and Benjamin.

4 This is what the LORD says: ‘Do not go and fight your brothers. Every one of you must return home. These words come from Me.’” So the people obeyed the words of the LORD. They turned back and did not fight against Jeroboam.
5 Rehoboam stayed in Jerusalem. He built strong cities to defend Judah.
6 He built up Bethlehem, Etam, Tekoa,
7 Beth-zur, Soco, Adullam,
8 Gath, Mareshah, Ziph,
9 Adoraim, Lachish, Azekah,
10 Zorah, Aijalon, and Hebron. These were the strong cities in Judah and Benjamin.
11 He made these strong places even stronger. He put officers in them. He gave them food, oil, and wine.
12 He also put shields and spears in all the cities. He made them very strong. So Judah and Benjamin belonged to him.
13 The priests and Levites from all over Israel came to Rehoboam. They stood with him.
14 The Levites left their open fields and their land. They went to Judah and Jerusalem. Jeroboam and his sons would not let them serve as priests of the LORD.
15 Jeroboam chose his own priests instead. They served at the high places. They served the goat demons and calf idols that he had made.
16 Some people from every tribe of Israel really wanted to follow the LORD their God. They came after the Levites to Jerusalem. They came to offer sacrifices to the LORD, the God of their fathers.
17 So they made the kingdom of Judah strong. They supported Rehoboam son of Solomon for three years. For three years they lived as David and Solomon had lived.
18 Rehoboam married Mahalath. She was the daughter of Jerimoth, son of David. Her mother was Abihail, daughter of Eliab son of Jesse.
19 She had three sons by him: Jeush, Shemariah, and Zaham.
20 After her, Rehoboam married Maacah daughter of Absalom. She had sons by him: Abijah, Attai, Ziza, and Shelomith.
21 Rehoboam loved Maacah more than all his other wives and extra wives. He had eighteen wives and sixty extra wives. He was the father of twenty-eight sons and sixty daughters.
22 Rehoboam chose Abijah son of Maacah as chief leader among his brothers. He planned to make him the next king.

23 Rehoboam did a wise thing. He sent some of his sons to live in many areas of Judah and Benjamin. He sent them to all the strong cities. He gave them plenty of food and supplies. He also found many wives for them.

title: 2 Chronicles 12
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 After Rehoboam became strong as king, he and all Israel turned away from the Law of the LORD.
2 In the fifth year of Rehoboam's reign, Shishak king of Egypt came up and attacked Jerusalem, because they were not faithful to the LORD.
3 He came with 1,200 chariots and 60,000 horsemen. With him came soldiers too many to count: Libyans, Sukkites, and Cushites.
4 He captured the strong, walled cities of Judah and came as far as Jerusalem.

5 Then Shemaiah the prophet came to Rehoboam and the leaders of Judah. They had gathered at Jerusalem because of Shishak. He said to them, "This is what the LORD says: 'You have forsaken Me; so I have given you into the hand of Shishak.'"
6 So the leaders of Israel and the king humbled themselves. They said, "The LORD is right and just."

7 When the LORD saw that they had humbled themselves, the word of the LORD came to Shemaiah. He said, "They have humbled themselves. I will not destroy them. I will soon rescue them. My anger will not fall on Jerusalem through Shishak.
8 But they will become his servants. Then they will learn the difference between serving Me and serving the kings of other lands."
9 So King Shishak of Egypt attacked Jerusalem. He took the treasures of the house of the LORD and of the royal palace. He took everything, even the gold shields that Solomon had made.
10 Then King Rehoboam made bronze shields in their place. He gave them to the care of the leaders of the guards at the entrance to the royal palace.
11 Whenever the king entered the house of the LORD, the guards went with him. They carried the shields. Later they brought them back to the guardroom.
12 Because Rehoboam humbled himself, the anger of the LORD turned away from him. He did not destroy him completely. Indeed, things were good in Judah.

13 So King Rehoboam became strong in Jerusalem. He was forty-one years old when he became king. He reigned seventeen years in Jerusalem, the city the LORD had chosen from all the tribes of Israel to put His Name there. His mother's name was Naamah the Ammonite.
14 Rehoboam did evil, because he did not choose in his heart to seek the LORD.

15 Now the acts of Rehoboam, from first to last, are written in the records of Shemaiah the Prophet and of Iddo the Seer about the family records. There was war between Rehoboam and Jeroboam all their days.
16 Then Rehoboam died and joined his fathers, and he was buried in the City of David. His son Abijah became king in his place.

title: 2 Chronicles 13
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 In the eighteenth year of Jeroboam's reign, Abijah became king of Judah.
2 He reigned in Jerusalem for three years. His mother was Micaiah, daughter of Uriel. She was from Gibeah. There was war between Abijah and Jeroboam.
3 Abijah went to battle with 400,000 chosen men. Jeroboam lined up against him with 800,000 brave fighting men.
4 Then Abijah stood on Mount Zemaraim in the hill country of Ephraim. He said, "Listen to me, Jeroboam and all Israel!
5 Do you not know this? The LORD, the God of Israel, gave the kingship of Israel to David and his sons forever by a covenant of salt.
6 Yet Jeroboam son of Nebat, a servant of Solomon son of David, stood up and turned against his master.
7 Then bad and evil men joined him. They stood against Rehoboam son of Solomon when he was young and had no skill. He could not stand against them.

8 And now you think you can stand against the kingdom of the LORD. That kingdom is in the hands of David's sons. You are a huge army, and you have with you the golden calves that Jeroboam made as gods.

9 But did you not drive out the priests of the LORD, the sons of Aaron, and the Levites? Did you not make your own priests, like the peoples of other lands? Now anyone who comes to set himself apart with a young bull and seven rams can become a priest of things that are not gods.
10 But for us, the LORD is our God. We have not left Him. The priests who serve the LORD are sons of Aaron, and the Levites do their duties.

11 Every morning and every evening they offer burnt offerings and sweet-smelling incense to the LORD. They set out the rows of showbread on the clean holy table. Every evening they light the lamps of the gold lampstand. We do what the LORD our God asks, but you have left Him.

12 Look, God Himself is with us as our head. His priests have trumpets to blow the call for battle against you. O sons of Israel, do not fight against the LORD, the God of your fathers. You will not win."
13 Now Jeroboam had sent some troops behind Judah for a hidden attack. So he was in front of Judah, and the hidden troops were behind them.
14 When Judah turned and saw that the battle was both in front and behind, they cried out to the LORD. Then the priests blew the trumpets,
15 and the men of Judah raised the battle cry. When they raised the cry, God defeated Jeroboam and all Israel before Abijah and Judah.
16 So the Israelites ran away from Judah, and God gave them into the hands of Judah.
17 Then Abijah and his people hit them with great force. 500,000 chosen men of Israel were killed.
18 So the Israelites were brought low at that time, and the men of Judah won. They won because they trusted the LORD, the God of their fathers.
19 Abijah chased Jeroboam and took some cities from him: Bethel, Jeshanah, and Ephron, along with their villages.
20 Jeroboam did not get his power back during the days of Abijah. The LORD struck him down, and he died.
21 But Abijah grew strong. He married fourteen wives and became the father of twenty-two sons and sixteen daughters.
22 The rest of the acts of Abijah, his ways, and his words, are written in the Book of the Prophet Iddo.

title: 2 Chronicles 14
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 Then Abijah died like his fathers, and they buried him in the City of David. His son Asa became king after him. In Asa's days the land had peace for ten years.
2 Asa did what was good and right in the eyes of the LORD his God.
3 He took away the foreign altars and the high places. He broke the holy stone pillars and cut down the Asherah poles.
4 He told the people of Judah to seek the LORD, the God of their fathers, and to obey the law and the commandments.
5 He also took away the high places and the altars for burning incense from every city in Judah. Under his rule the kingdom had peace.
6 Because the land had peace, Asa built strong walled cities in Judah. In those days no one made war with him, because the LORD had given him rest.

7 So he said to the people of Judah, "Let us build these cities. Let us put walls and towers around them, with doors and bars. The land is still ours because we have sought the LORD our God. We have sought Him, and He has given us rest on every side." So they built and did well.
8 Asa had an army of 300,000 men from Judah with large shields and spears. He also had 280,000 men from Benjamin with small shields and bows. All of them were brave and strong soldiers.
9 Then Zerah the Cushite came against them with an army of 1,000,000 men and 300 chariots. They came as far as Mareshah.
10 So Asa marched out to meet him. He set up his army for battle in the Valley of Zephathah near Mareshah.

11 Then Asa cried out to the LORD his God: "O LORD, only You can help the weak against the strong. Help us, O LORD our God, for we trust You. In Your name we have come against this huge army. O LORD, You are our God. Do not let a mere man win against You."
12 So the LORD defeated the Cushites in front of Asa and Judah, and the Cushites ran away.
13 Then Asa and his army chased them as far as Gerar. The Cushites fell and could not get up, for they were broken by the LORD and His army. The people of Judah carried away much plunder.
14 They also attacked all the cities around Gerar, because a great fear of the LORD came on them. They took everything from these cities, because there were many good things to take.
15 They also attacked the tents of the men who kept animals. They took away many sheep and camels. Then they returned to Jerusalem.

title: 2 Chronicles 15
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 The Spirit of God came on Azariah son of Oded.

2 He went out to meet Asa and said, "Listen to me, Asa and all Judah and Benjamin. The LORD is with you when you are with Him. If you seek Him, you will find Him. But if you leave Him, He will leave you.
3 For many years Israel had no true God. They had no priest to teach them. They had no law.
4 But when they were in trouble, they turned to the LORD, the God of Israel. They looked for Him, and they found Him.
5 In those days it was not safe to travel. The people in the lands had many fights.
6 Nation crushed nation, and city crushed city. God sent them many troubles.
7 But you must be strong. Do not lose hope. Your work will be rewarded."

8 Asa heard these words and the message from Azariah the prophet. He took courage. He removed the hated idols from all Judah and Benjamin. He also removed them from the towns he had taken in the hills of Ephraim. Then he repaired the altar of the LORD in front of the LORD's temple.
9 Asa gathered all Judah and Benjamin. People from Ephraim, Manasseh, and Simeon were living among them. Many had come over to him from Israel when they saw the LORD his God was with him.
10 They came together in Jerusalem in the third month of the fifteenth year of Asa's rule.
11 That day they offered to the LORD seven hundred oxen and seven thousand sheep. These were taken from what they had brought back in war.
12 They made a covenant to seek the LORD, the God of their fathers, with all their heart and soul.
13 Anyone who would not seek the LORD, the God of Israel, would be killed. This was for young or old, man or woman.
14 They made a promise to the LORD with a loud voice. They shouted with trumpets and horns from male sheep.
15 All Judah was very glad about the promise. They had made it with all their heart. They looked for Him with all their heart, and they found Him. So the LORD gave them peace on every side.

16 King Asa also removed his grandmother Maacah from her place as the king's mother. She had made a hated Asherah pole (a wooden idol). Asa cut it down, crushed it, and burned it in the Kidron Valley.
17 The high places of worship were not removed from Israel. But Asa's heart was fully given to the LORD all his days.
18 He brought into the house of God the silver and gold things that he and his father had given to God.
19 There was no war until the thirty-fifth year of Asa's rule.

title: 2 Chronicles 16
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 In the 36th year of Asa's reign, Baasha king of Israel came up against Judah. He built strong walls at Ramah to stop anyone from going in or out of Asa's land.
2 So Asa took silver and gold from the storage rooms of the house of the LORD and the royal palace. He sent it with this message to Ben-hadad king of Aram, who ruled in Damascus:

3 "Let there be a treaty between me and you, as there was between my father and your father. See, I have sent you silver and gold. Now break your treaty with Baasha king of Israel, so he will leave me alone."
4 Ben-hadad listened to King Asa. He sent his army leaders against the cities of Israel. They took Ijon, Dan, Abel-maim, and all the supply cities of Naphtali.
5 When Baasha heard this, he stopped building at Ramah and left his work.
6 Then King Asa brought all the men of Judah. They carried away the stones and wood that Baasha had used. With these things, Asa built up Geba and Mizpah.
7 At that time Hanani the seer came to King Asa of Judah. He said, "You trusted in the king of Aram, not in the LORD your God. So the army of the king of Aram has slipped from your hand.
8 Were not the Cushites and Libyans a huge army with many war carts and horse soldiers? Yet because you trusted in the LORD, He gave them into your hand.
9 The eyes of the LORD look back and forth over all the earth. He shows His strength for those whose hearts are fully given to Him. You have acted foolishly in this. From now on, you will have wars."
10 Asa was angry with the seer. He was so angry that he put the man in prison. At that time Asa also treated some of the people badly.
11 The rest of the acts of Asa, from start to end, are written in the Book of the Kings of Judah and Israel.
12 In the 39th year of his reign, Asa became sick in his feet. His sickness grew worse and worse. Yet even when he was sick, he did not seek the LORD. He sought only the doctors.
13 So in the 41st year of his reign, Asa died and joined his ancestors in death.
14 They buried him in the tomb that he had cut for himself in the City of David. They laid him on a bed for the dead full of spices and mixed sweet oils. Then they made a great fire in his honor.

title: 2 Chronicles 17
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 Asa's son Jehoshaphat became king after him. He made himself strong against Israel.
2 He placed soldiers in every strong walled city of Judah. He also put groups of soldiers in the land of Judah and in the cities of Ephraim that his father Asa had taken.
3 The LORD was with Jehoshaphat. He walked in the early ways of his father David. He did not look to the Baals.
4 Instead, he sought the God of his father. He walked by God's commandments and did not follow the ways of Israel.
5 So the LORD made the kingdom strong in his hand. All Judah brought him gifts. He had great riches and honor.
6 His heart was glad in the ways of the LORD. He also removed the high places and Asherah poles from Judah.
7 In the third year of his reign, Jehoshaphat sent his officials to teach in the cities of Judah. They were Ben-hail, Obadiah, Zechariah, Nethanel, and Micaiah.
8 With them went some Levites: Shemaiah, Nethaniah, Zebadiah, Asahel, Shemiramoth, Jehonathan, Adonijah, Tobijah, and Tob-adonijah. The priests Elishama and Jehoram went too.
9 They taught all through Judah. They took the Book of the Law of the LORD with them. They went to the towns of Judah and taught the people.
10 The fear of the LORD fell on all the kingdoms around Judah. So they did not make war against Jehoshaphat.
11 Some Philistines brought gifts and silver to Jehoshaphat. The Arabs brought him 7,700 rams and 7,700 goats from their flocks.
12 Jehoshaphat grew stronger and stronger. He built strong forts and storage cities in Judah.
13 He kept large supplies in the cities of Judah. He also had warriors in Jerusalem who were mighty men of valor.
14 These are their numbers by their family houses. From Judah, the leaders of thousands were: Adnah the commander, and with him 300,000 mighty men of valor;
15 next to him, Jehohanan the commander, with 280,000 men;
16 and next to him, Amasiah son of Zichri, who gave himself to the LORD, with 200,000 mighty men of valor.
17 From Benjamin: Eliada, a mighty man of valor, with 200,000 men armed with bows and shields;
18 and next to him, Jehozabad, with 180,000 men ready for battle.
19 These men served the king. There were also more men he had placed in the strong walled cities throughout Judah.

title: 2 Chronicles 18
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 Jehoshaphat had great riches and honor. He joined Ahab's family by marriage.
2 Some years later he went down to visit Ahab in Samaria. Ahab killed many sheep and cattle for him and his people. Ahab pushed him to go up and fight at Ramoth-gilead.
3 Ahab king of Israel asked Jehoshaphat king of Judah, "Will you go with me against Ramoth-gilead?" Jehoshaphat replied, "I am like you, and my people are your people. We will join you in the war."
4 But Jehoshaphat also said to the king of Israel, "Please ask the LORD first for His word."

5 So the king of Israel called the prophets together, four hundred men. He asked them, "Should we go to war against Ramoth-gilead, or should we hold back?" "Go up," they replied, "and God will give it to the king."
6 But Jehoshaphat asked, "Is there not still a prophet of the LORD here? We can ask him too."

7 The king of Israel answered, "There is still one man who can ask the LORD. But I hate him. He never speaks anything good for me, only bad. He is Micaiah son of Imlah." "The king should not say that!" Jehoshaphat replied.
8 So the king of Israel called one of his officials. He said, "Bring Micaiah son of Imlah at once."
9 The king of Israel and Jehoshaphat king of Judah wore their royal clothes. They sat on their thrones at the open ground by the gate of Samaria. All the prophets were speaking before them.
10 Zedekiah son of Chenaanah had made iron horns for himself. He declared, "This is what the LORD says: 'With these you will stab the Arameans until they are finished.'"
11 All the prophets were saying the same thing. They said, "Go up to Ramoth-gilead and you will win. The LORD will give it to the king."
12 The messenger who went to call Micaiah told him, "Look, all the prophets together speak good words to the king. Please let your words be like theirs. Speak good words too."
13 But Micaiah said, "The LORD lives, and I promise this: I will say only what my God tells me."
14 When Micaiah arrived, the king asked him, "Micaiah, should we go to war against Ramoth-gilead, or should we hold back?" "Go up and win," Micaiah replied. "They will be given into your hand."
15 But the king said to him, "How many times must I make you promise to tell me only the truth in the name of the LORD?"
16 So Micaiah said, "I saw all Israel scattered on the hills like sheep with no shepherd. And the LORD said, 'These people have no master. Let each one go home in peace.'"
17 Then the king of Israel said to Jehoshaphat, "Did I not tell you? He never speaks good for me, only bad."
18 Micaiah continued, "So hear the word of the LORD. I saw the LORD sitting on His throne. All the armies of heaven stood on His right and on His left.
19 And the LORD said, 'Who will trick Ahab king of Israel into going up to fall at Ramoth-gilead?' One said this, and another said that.
20 Then a spirit came forward and stood before the LORD. It said, 'I will trick him.' 'How?' asked the LORD.
21 And the spirit replied, 'I will go out and be a lying spirit in the mouths of all his prophets.' The LORD said, 'You will trick him and win. Go and do it.'
22 So you see, the LORD has put a lying spirit in the mouths of these prophets of yours. The LORD has said that trouble will come on you."
23 Then Zedekiah son of Chenaanah went up and hit Micaiah in the face. He demanded, "Which way did the Spirit of the LORD go when He left me to speak with you?"
24 Micaiah replied, "You will see on that day. You will go and hide in an inner room."
25 And the king of Israel said, "Take Micaiah back to Amon the city governor and to Joash the king's son.
26 Tell them this is what the king says: 'Put this man in prison. Give him only bread and water until I return safely.'"
27 But Micaiah replied, "If you return safely, the LORD has not spoken through me." Then he added, "Listen carefully, all you people!"
28 So the king of Israel and Jehoshaphat king of Judah went up to Ramoth-gilead.
29 The king of Israel said to Jehoshaphat, "I will change my clothes to hide who I am, then go into battle. But you wear your royal robes." So the king of Israel hid who he was and went into battle.
30 Now the king of Aram had given an order to his chariot commanders. He said, "Do not fight anyone, small or great, except the king of Israel."
31 When the chariot commanders saw Jehoshaphat, they said, "This is the king of Israel!" So they turned to fight him. But Jehoshaphat cried out, and the LORD helped him. God drew them away from him.
32 When the chariot commanders saw that he was not the king of Israel, they turned back and stopped chasing him.
33 But a man drew his bow without aiming at anyone. He hit the king of Israel through a gap in his armor. So the king said to his driver, "Turn around and take me out of the battle. I am badly hurt!"
34 The fighting was very hard all that day. The king of Israel held himself up in his chariot, facing the Arameans, until evening. At sunset he died.

title: 2 Chronicles 19
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 King Jehoshaphat of Judah came back safely to his home in Jerusalem.

2 Then Jehu son of Hanani, the prophet, went out to meet him. He said to King Jehoshaphat, "Should you help evil people? Should you love those who hate the LORD? Because of this, the LORD's anger is on you.
3 But some good is found in you. You took the Asherah poles out of the land. You tried hard to seek God."
4 Jehoshaphat lived in Jerusalem. He went out again among the people, from Beersheba to the hill country of Ephraim. He turned them back to the LORD, the God of their fathers.
5 He chose judges in the land. He placed them in each strong city of Judah with walls.
6 Then he said to the judges, "Think carefully about what you do. You are not judging for man, but for the LORD. He is with you when you give a decision.
7 Now let the fear of the LORD be upon you. Be careful in what you do. With the LORD our God there are no unfair acts, no favorites, and no money for wrong decisions."
8 Jehoshaphat also chose some of the Levites, priests, and heads of the families of Israel. They lived in Jerusalem. They were to judge for the LORD and to decide arguments.
9 He commanded them, "You must serve faithfully and with all your heart in the fear of the LORD.

10 Cases will come to you from your brothers who live in their cities. Some will be about killing. Others will be about rules and laws and commands. You must warn the people. Then they will not become guilty before the LORD. Then anger will not come on you and on your brothers. Do this, and you will not become guilty.

11 Look, Amariah the head priest will be over you in all matters of the LORD. Zebadiah son of Ishmael, the leader of Judah's family, will be over you in all the king's matters. The Levites will serve as officers before you. Be strong and act! May the LORD be with the good people!"

title: 2 Chronicles 20
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 After this, the Moabites, the Ammonites, and some Meunites came to make war against Jehoshaphat.
2 Then some men came and told Jehoshaphat, "A very large army is coming against you from Edom, from beyond the Sea. They are already in Hazazon-tamar" (that is, En-gedi).
3 Jehoshaphat was very afraid. He turned to seek the LORD. He called a fast in all of Judah.
4 So the people of Judah gathered to seek the LORD. They came from all the cities of Judah to seek Him.
5 Then Jehoshaphat stood in the meeting of Judah and Jerusalem. He stood in the house of the LORD, in front of the new courtyard.
6 He said, "O LORD, God of our fathers, are You not the God in heaven? Do You not rule over all the kingdoms of the nations? Power and might are in Your hand. No one can stand against You.
7 Our God, did You not drive out the people of this land before Your people Israel? Did You not give it forever to the family of Abraham Your friend?
8 They have lived in the land. They have built a holy house for Your Name. They said,

9 'If trouble comes upon us—the sword or judgment, sickness or hunger—we will stand before this temple and before You. Your Name is in this temple. We will cry out to You in our trouble, and You will hear us and save us.'
10 And now, here are the men of Ammon, Moab, and Mount Seir. You did not let Israel invade them when Israel came out of Egypt. Israel turned away from them and did not destroy them.
11 See how they pay us back! They come to drive us out of the land You gave us as a gift.
12 Our God, will You not judge them? **For we have no power against this great multitude … but our eyes are upon You**."
13 Meanwhile all the men of Judah stood before the LORD. Their wives, children, and little ones stood with them.

14 Then the Spirit of the LORD came upon Jahaziel. He was the son of Zechariah, the son of Benaiah, the son of Jeiel, the son of Mattaniah. He was a Levite from Asaph's family. He stood in the middle of the meeting.

15 He said, "Listen, all you people of Judah and Jerusalem! Listen, King Jehoshaphat! This is what the LORD says: 'Do not be afraid or upset because of this very large army. **The battle is not yours, but God's**.
16 Tomorrow you must march down against them. You will see them coming up the Ascent of Ziz. You will find them at the end of the valley facing the Wilderness of Jeruel.

17 You will not need to fight this battle. Take your places, stand firm, and see the saving help of the LORD. Do not be afraid or upset, O Judah and Jerusalem. Go out and face them tomorrow, for the LORD is with you.'"
18 Then Jehoshaphat bowed his face to the ground. All the people of Judah and Jerusalem fell down before the LORD to worship Him.
19 The Levites from the Kohathites and Korahites stood up. They praised the LORD, the God of Israel, with a very loud voice.

20 Early in the morning they got up and left for the Wilderness of Tekoa. As they set out, Jehoshaphat stood up and said, "Hear me, O people of Judah and Jerusalem. Believe in the LORD your God, and you will stand strong. Believe in His prophets, and you will succeed."

21 Then Jehoshaphat talked with the people. He chose those who would sing to the LORD and praise the beauty of His holiness. They went out before the army, singing: "Give thanks to the LORD, for His faithful love lasts forever."
22 The moment they began their shouts and praises, the LORD set hidden attacks against the men of Ammon, Moab, and Mount Seir. These men had come against Judah, and they were defeated.
23 The Ammonites and Moabites rose up against the people of Mount Seir. They destroyed them fully. When they had finished off the people of Seir, they helped to destroy one another.
24 When the men of Judah came to a place that looked over the wilderness, they searched for the very large army. But only dead bodies lay on the ground. No one had escaped.

25 Then Jehoshaphat and his people went to take the things left behind. They found on the bodies many goods and valuable things—more than they could carry away. They gathered the things for three days because there was so much.
26 On the fourth day they gathered in the Valley of Beracah. There they blessed the LORD. So that place is called the Valley of Beracah to this day.
27 Then all the men of Judah and Jerusalem returned to Jerusalem with joy, with Jehoshaphat at their head. The LORD had made them glad over their enemies.
28 So they entered Jerusalem and went into the house of the LORD with harps, lyres, and trumpets.
29 The fear of God came upon all the kingdoms of the lands when they heard that the LORD had fought against the enemies of Israel.
30 Then Jehoshaphat's kingdom was at peace. His God had given him rest on every side.
31 So Jehoshaphat reigned over Judah. He was thirty-five years old when he became king. He reigned in Jerusalem twenty-five years. His mother's name was Azubah daughter of Shilhi.
32 Jehoshaphat walked in the way of his father Asa. He did not turn away from it. He did what was right in the eyes of the LORD.
33 But the high places were not removed. The people had not yet set their hearts on the God of their fathers.
34 As for the rest of the acts of Jehoshaphat, from beginning to end, they are written in the Chronicles of Jehu son of Hanani. These are recorded in the Book of the Kings of Israel.
35 Later, Jehoshaphat king of Judah joined together with Ahaziah king of Israel, who did evil.
36 They agreed to make ships to go to Tarshish. These ships were built in Ezion-geber.

37 Then Eliezer son of Dodavahu of Mareshah spoke God's word against Jehoshaphat. He said, "Because you have joined with Ahaziah, the LORD has destroyed your works." So the ships were wrecked and could not sail to Tarshish.

title: 2 Chronicles 21
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 Jehoshaphat died like his fathers before him. He was buried with them in the City of David. His son Jehoram became king after him.
2 Jehoram's brothers, the sons of Jehoshaphat, were Azariah, Jehiel, Zechariah, Azariah, Michael, and Shephatiah. All of these were sons of Jehoshaphat king of Israel.
3 Their father had given them many gifts of silver, gold, and costly treasures. He also gave them strong cities with walls in Judah. But he gave the kingdom to Jehoram because he was the firstborn.
4 When Jehoram took his father's kingdom, he made himself strong. He killed all his brothers with the sword. He also killed some of the leaders of Israel.
5 Jehoram was thirty-two years old when he became king. He ruled in Jerusalem for eight years.
6 Jehoram lived the same way as the kings of Israel. He did just like the house of Ahab. He married a daughter of Ahab. He did evil before the LORD.
7 Yet the LORD did not want to destroy the house of David. The LORD had made a covenant with David. He had promised to keep a lamp for David and his family forever.
8 In the days of Jehoram, Edom broke free from Judah's rule. They chose their own king.
9 So Jehoram crossed into Edom with his officers and all his chariots. The Edomites came around him and his chariot leaders. He got up and attacked them at night.
10 So to this day, Edom has stayed free from Judah's rule. At the same time, Libnah also broke free from Jehoram. This happened because Jehoram had turned away from the LORD, the God of his fathers.
11 Jehoram had also built high places on the hills of Judah. He led the people of Jerusalem to be unfaithful to God. He led Judah away from God.

12 Then a letter came to Jehoram from Elijah the prophet. It said: "This is what the LORD, the God of your father David, says: 'You have not lived the same way as your father Jehoshaphat or as Asa king of Judah.

13 Instead, you have lived the same way as the kings of Israel. You have led Judah and the people of Jerusalem to be unfaithful to God, just as the house of Ahab did. You have also killed your brothers, your father's family. They were better than you.
14 So look, the LORD is about to hit your people with great trouble. He will hit your sons, your wives, and all your things.
15 And day after day you will suffer from a bad sickness. It will be a sickness inside your body. It will get worse until your insides come out.'"
16 Then the LORD turned the hearts of the Philistines and Arabs against Jehoram. These Arabs lived near the Cushites.

17 They went to war against Judah and entered the land. They took away all the things in the king's palace. They also took his sons and wives. Not one son was left to him, except Jehoahaz, his youngest.
18 After all this, the LORD made Jehoram sick with a disease in his body that could not be healed.

19 This went on day after day until two full years had passed. Finally, his insides came out because of his disease. He died in great pain. His people did not make a fire in his honor, as they had done for his fathers.
20 Jehoram was thirty-two years old when he became king. He ruled in Jerusalem for eight years. He died, and no one was sad. He was buried in the City of David, but not in the royal graves.

title: 2 Chronicles 22
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 Then the people of Jerusalem made Ahaziah king. He was the youngest son of Jehoram. Groups of attackers had come into the camp with the Arabs. They had killed all the older sons. So Ahaziah son of Jehoram became king of Judah.
2 Ahaziah was twenty-two years old when he became king. He ruled in Jerusalem for one year. His mother’s name was Athaliah. She was the granddaughter of Omri.
3 Ahaziah also walked in the ways of the house of Ahab. His mother pushed him to do evil.
4 He did evil in the sight of the LORD, like the house of Ahab. After his father died, they were his advisers. This led to his ruin.
5 Ahaziah followed their advice. He went with Joram son of Ahab, king of Israel. They went to fight Hazael, king of Aram, at Ramoth-gilead. But the Arameans hurt Joram in the battle.

6 So Joram returned to Jezreel to get better. They had hurt him at Ramah, when he fought Hazael king of Aram. Then Ahaziah son of Jehoram, king of Judah, went down to Jezreel. He went to visit Joram son of Ahab, because Joram was hurt.

7 Ahaziah’s fall came from God. It came when he went to visit Joram. When Ahaziah arrived, he went out with Joram. They went to meet Jehu son of Nimshi. The LORD had anointed Jehu to destroy the house of Ahab.
8 Jehu was carrying out God's punishment on the house of Ahab. He found the rulers of Judah. He also found the sons of Ahaziah’s brothers who served Ahaziah. Jehu killed them.

9 Then Jehu looked for Ahaziah. Jehu’s soldiers caught him while he was hiding in Samaria. They brought Ahaziah to Jehu and killed him. They buried him. They said, “He is the grandson of Jehoshaphat, who followed the LORD with all his heart.” So no one from the house of Ahaziah was strong enough to rule the kingdom.
10 Athaliah was the mother of Ahaziah. When she saw that her son was dead, she started to kill all the sons of the royal family of Judah.

11 But Jehoshabeath was the daughter of King Jehoram. She took Joash son of Ahaziah. She stole him away from the king’s sons who were being killed. She put him and his nurse in a bedroom. Jehoshabeath was the daughter of King Jehoram. She was also the wife of Jehoiada the priest. She was Ahaziah’s sister. She hid Joash from Athaliah, so Athaliah could not kill him.
12 Joash stayed hidden with them in the house of God for six years. During that time, Athaliah ruled the land.

title: 2 Chronicles 23
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 In the seventh year, Jehoiada the priest grew strong. He made a covenant with the leaders of hundreds. Their names were Azariah son of Jeroham, Ishmael son of Jehohanan, Azariah son of Obed, Maaseiah son of Adaiah, and Elishaphat son of Zichri.
2 They went through all of Judah. They gathered the Levites from every city of Judah. They also gathered the heads of the families of Israel. Then they all came to Jerusalem.
3 The whole group made a covenant with the king in the house of God. Jehoiada said, “Look, here is the king’s son! He must rule as king. The LORD promised this about the family of David.”
4 “This is what you must do. One third of you priests and Levites who come on duty on the Sabbath will guard the doors.
5 Another third will guard the royal palace. Another third will guard the Foundation Gate. All the other people will stay in the courtyards of the house of the LORD.
6 No one may enter the house of the LORD. Only the priests and the Levites who serve may enter. They may enter because they are set apart for God. All the other people must obey the rule of the LORD.
7 The Levites must stand all around the king with weapons in their hands. Anyone who enters the temple must be killed. Stay close to the king wherever he goes.”

8 So the Levites and all the people of Judah did what Jehoiada the priest had said. Each leader took his men. He took those starting duty on the Sabbath and those finishing duty. For Jehoiada the priest had not let any of the groups go home.
9 Then Jehoiada the priest gave the leaders of hundreds the spears and shields of King David. They were the large and small shields kept in the house of God.
10 He placed all the soldiers around the king. Each one had his weapon in his hand. They stood by the altar and the temple, from the south side to the north side.

11 Then Jehoiada and his sons brought out the king’s son. They put the crown on his head. They gave him the book of the Law. They made him king. They anointed him. Then they shouted, “Long live the king!”
12 Athaliah heard the noise of the people running and cheering the king. So she went out to them in the house of the LORD.

13 She looked and saw the king standing by his pillar at the entrance. The officers and trumpet players stood beside the king. All the people of the land were full of joy and blowing trumpets. The singers with their instruments were leading the praises. Then Athaliah tore her clothes and screamed, “You are traitors! Traitors!”

14 Jehoiada the priest sent out the leaders of hundreds in charge of the army. He said, “Bring her out between the lines. Kill with the sword anyone who follows her.” For the priest had said, “She must not be killed in the house of the LORD.”
15 So they grabbed Athaliah as she reached the entrance of the Horse Gate at the palace. There they killed her.
16 Then Jehoiada made a covenant between himself, the king, and the people. They agreed that they would be the LORD’s people.
17 So all the people went to the temple of Baal and tore it down. They broke the altars and idols into pieces. They also killed Mattan the priest of Baal in front of the altars.

18 Then Jehoiada put the care of the house of the LORD into the hands of the Levitical priests. David had chosen them to serve at the house of the LORD. They were to offer burnt offerings to the LORD as the Law of Moses says. They served with joy and song, as David had set in order.
19 He placed guards at the gates of the house of the LORD. So nothing unclean could enter for any reason.

20 He took with him the leaders of hundreds, the leaders of the people, and all the people of the land. They brought the king down from the house of the LORD. They went into the royal palace through the Upper Gate. They seated King Joash on the royal throne.
21 All the people of the land were full of joy. And the city was quiet, because Athaliah had been killed with the sword.

title: 2 Chronicles 24
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 Joash was seven years old when he became king. He ruled in Jerusalem for forty years. His mother was Zibiah from Beersheba.
2 Joash did what was right before the LORD. He did this all the days of Jehoiada the priest.
3 Jehoiada chose two wives for Joash. Joash had sons and daughters.
4 Some time later, Joash decided to fix the house of the LORD.
5 So he called the priests and Levites. He said, "Go to the cities of Judah. Collect the yearly money from all Israel to fix the house of your God. Do it quickly." But the Levites did not hurry.

6 So the king called Jehoiada the high priest. He said, "Why have you not made the Levites bring the tax from Judah and Jerusalem? This is the tax that Moses the servant of the LORD set for the Tent of the Testimony."
7 The sons of that wicked woman Athaliah had broken into the house of God. They had even used the holy things of the LORD's house for the Baals.
8 So at the king's command, they made a chest. They placed it outside, at the gate of the LORD's house.
9 They made an announcement in Judah and Jerusalem. The people were to bring the tax that Moses the servant of God set for Israel in the wilderness.
10 All the officers and people were glad. They brought their gifts and put them in the chest until it was full.

11 The Levites would bring the chest to the king's officers. When they saw a large amount of money, the king's writer and the high priest's officer would come. They would empty the chest and carry it back. They did this daily and gathered much money.

12 Then the king and Jehoiada gave the money to the men who watched over the work. They hired stone workers and wood workers to fix the house of the LORD. They also hired iron workers and bronze workers for the repairs.
13 So the workers labored, and the work went well in their hands. They fixed the house of God as it was first made. They made it strong.

14 When they finished, they brought the rest of the money to the king and Jehoiada. They used it to make tools for the house of the LORD. These were tools for the service and for burnt offerings, dishes, and other gold and silver things. All the days of Jehoiada, burnt offerings were brought to the house of the LORD often.
15 Jehoiada grew old and full of years. He died at the age of 130.
16 They buried him with the kings in the City of David. This was because he had done good in Israel for God and His temple.
17 After Jehoiada died, the officials of Judah came to the king. They bowed down before him, and he listened to them.
18 They left the house of the LORD, the God of their fathers. They served the Asherah poles and idols. So anger came upon Judah and Jerusalem because of their sin.
19 Yet the LORD sent prophets to bring the people back to Him. The prophets warned them, but they would not listen.

20 Then the Spirit of God came upon Zechariah son of Jehoiada the priest. He stood before the people and said, "This is what God says: 'Why do you break the LORD's commands? You cannot prosper this way. Because you have left the LORD, He has left you.'"
21 But they planned to kill Zechariah. By order of the king, they killed him with stones in the courtyard of the LORD's house.
22 King Joash did not remember the kindness of Jehoiada, Zechariah's father. Instead, Joash killed Jehoiada's son. As he was dying, Zechariah said, "May the LORD see this and make you pay for it."
23 In the spring, the army of Aram came to fight Joash. They entered Judah and Jerusalem. They destroyed all the leaders of the people. They sent all the stolen goods to their king in Damascus.
24 The Aramean army had come with only a few men. But the LORD handed over a very great army to them. Because Judah had left the LORD, the God of their fathers, judgment came on Joash.

25 When the Arameans left, they left Joash badly wounded. His own servants planned against him. They killed him for the blood of Jehoiada's son. They killed him on his bed. So he died and was buried in the City of David. But he was not buried in the tombs of the kings.
26 Those who planned against Joash were Zabad son of Shimeath the Ammonitess and Jehozabad son of Shimrith the Moabitess.
27 The records of the sons of Joash, the many messages about him, and the rebuilding of the house of God are written in the Book of the Kings. His son Amaziah became king in his place.

title: 2 Chronicles 25
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 Amaziah was twenty-five years old when he became king. He ruled in Jerusalem for twenty-nine years. His mother's name was Jehoaddan, from Jerusalem.
2 He did what was right in the eyes of the LORD. But he did not do it with his whole heart.
3 As soon as his kingdom was strong, Amaziah put to death the servants who had killed his father the king.

4 But he did not put their sons to death. He acted as it is written in the Law, in the Book of Moses. There the LORD commanded: "Fathers must not be put to death for their children. Children must not be put to death for their fathers. Each one must die for his own sin."

5 Then Amaziah gathered the people of Judah. He set them in groups by their families, under commanders of thousands and of hundreds. He counted all men twenty years old or older in Judah and Benjamin. He found 300,000 chosen men able to serve in the army with spear and shield.
6 He also hired 100,000 strong soldiers from Israel for a hundred talents of silver.
7 But a man of God came to him and said, "O king, do not let the army of Israel go with you. The LORD is not with Israel, not with any of the Ephraimites.
8 Even if you go and fight bravely, God will make you fall before the enemy. For God has power to help and power to bring down."
9 Amaziah asked the man of God, "But what about the hundred talents I gave to the army of Israel?" The man of God answered, "The LORD is able to give you much more than this."
10 So Amaziah sent home the troops from Ephraim. They were very angry with Judah and went home in great anger.
