11 Then Amaziah found his strength. He led his troops to the Valley of Salt. There he struck down 10,000 men of Seir.
12 The army of Judah also captured 10,000 men alive. They took them to the top of a cliff and threw them down. So all were broken to pieces.
13 Meanwhile the troops that Amaziah had sent away attacked the towns of Judah. They came from Samaria to Beth-horon. They struck down 3,000 people and carried off many stolen goods.
14 When Amaziah came back from killing the Edomites, he brought back the gods of the men of Seir. He set them up as his own gods, bowed down to them, and offered sacrifices to them by fire.
15 So the LORD became very angry with Amaziah. He sent a prophet to him, who said, "Why have you turned to this people's gods? They could not even save their own people from your hand."

16 While the prophet was still speaking, the king said, "Have we made you the king's adviser? Stop! Do you want to be struck down?" So the prophet stopped. But he said, "I know that God has decided to destroy you, because you have done this and have not listened to my advice."
17 Then Amaziah king of Judah asked his advisers. He sent word to Jehoash king of Israel, son of Jehoahaz, the son of Jehu. He said, "Come, let us meet face to face."

18 But Jehoash king of Israel sent this reply to Amaziah king of Judah: "A thorn bush in Lebanon sent a message to a cedar tree in Lebanon. It said, 'Give your daughter to my son in marriage.' But a wild animal in Lebanon came by and stepped on the thorn bush.
19 You say, 'Look, I have beaten Edom.' Now your heart is proud and full of boasting. Stay at home. Why bring trouble on yourself so that you fall, you and Judah with you?"
20 But Amaziah would not listen. This came from God, to give them into the hand of Jehoash, because they had turned to the gods of Edom.
21 So Jehoash king of Israel went up. He and Amaziah king of Judah met face to face at Beth-shemesh in Judah.
22 And Judah was beaten badly by Israel. Every man ran away to his own home.

23 There at Beth-shemesh, Jehoash king of Israel captured Amaziah king of Judah, the son of Joash, the son of Jehoahaz. Then Jehoash brought him to Jerusalem. He broke down the wall of Jerusalem from the Ephraim Gate to the Corner Gate, a part of four hundred cubits.
24 He took all the gold and silver and all the things in the house of God with Obed-edom. He also took the treasures of the royal palace, and some hostages. Then he returned to Samaria.
25 Amaziah son of Joash king of Judah lived for fifteen years after the death of Jehoash son of Jehoahaz king of Israel.
26 The other acts of Amaziah, from beginning to end, are they not written in the Book of the Kings of Judah and Israel?
27 From the time Amaziah turned away from the LORD, a secret plan was made against him in Jerusalem. He ran away to Lachish. But men were sent after him to Lachish, and they killed him there.
28 They carried him back on horses and buried him with his fathers in the City of Judah.

title: 2 Chronicles 26
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 All the people of Judah took Uzziah, who was sixteen years old, and made him king in place of his father Amaziah.
2 Uzziah rebuilt the town of Eloth and gave it back to Judah after King Amaziah died and joined his ancestors.
3 Uzziah was sixteen years old when he became king, and he ruled in Jerusalem for fifty-two years. His mother's name was Jecoliah, and she was from Jerusalem.
4 He did what was right in the eyes of the LORD, just as his father Amaziah had done.
5 He sought God during the days of Zechariah, who taught him to fear God. As long as he sought the LORD, God gave him success.
6 Uzziah went out to fight a war against the Philistines. He broke down the walls of Gath, Jabneh, and Ashdod. Then he built cities near Ashdod and among the Philistines.
7 God helped him against the Philistines, against the Arabs who lived in Gur-baal, and against the Meunites.
8 The Ammonites brought gifts of money to Uzziah. His fame spread as far as the border of Egypt, because he had become very powerful.
9 Uzziah built towers in Jerusalem at the Corner Gate, the Valley Gate, and at the angle in the wall. He made them strong.

10 He had much livestock in the foothills and on the plain. So he built towers in the desert and dug many water tanks. He loved the soil, so he had farmers and grape farmers in the hill country and in the rich fields.
11 Uzziah had an army ready for battle. It went out to war in groups, as written down by Jeiel the scribe and Maaseiah the officer, under the leadership of Hananiah, one of the king's officers.
12 The total number of family leaders of the brave fighters was 2,600.
13 Under their command was an army of 307,500 men trained for war. They were a strong force to help the king against his enemies.
14 Uzziah gave the whole army shields, spears, helmets, armor, bows, and sling-stones.

15 In Jerusalem he made clever machines, designed by skilled men, to shoot arrows and throw large stones from the towers and corners. His fame spread far and wide, for he was helped very much and became powerful.
16 But when Uzziah became powerful, his proud heart led to his own ruin. He was not loyal to the LORD his God. He went into the temple of the LORD to burn incense on the altar of incense.
17 Then Azariah the priest went in after him with eighty brave priests of the LORD.

18 They stood against King Uzziah and said, "Uzziah, you have no right to offer incense to the LORD. Only the priests, the descendants of Aaron, are set apart to burn incense. Leave the holy place, for you have not been faithful. You will not receive honor from the LORD God."

19 Uzziah was holding a metal pan for incense in his hand. He became very angry. But while he raged at the priests in the house of the LORD before the altar of incense, leprosy (a skin disease) broke out on his forehead.
20 When Azariah the chief priest and all the priests turned to him and saw the leprosy on his forehead, they rushed him out. He himself also hurried to leave, because the LORD had struck him.

21 So King Uzziah had leprosy until the day he died. He lived alone, away from people, and was cut off from the house of the LORD. His son Jotham took charge of the royal palace and ruled the people of the land.
22 The rest of the acts of Uzziah, from beginning to end, were written down by the prophet Isaiah son of Amoz.
23 Uzziah died and joined his ancestors, and he was buried near them in a burial field that belonged to the kings. For the people said, "He was a leper." And his son Jotham became king in his place.

title: 2 Chronicles 27
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 Jotham was twenty-five years old when he became king. He was king in Jerusalem for sixteen years. His mother's name was Jerushah, the daughter of Zadok.
2 He did what the LORD said was right, just as his father Uzziah had done. But he did not go into the temple of the LORD. And the people still did bad things.
3 Jotham built the Upper Gate of the house of the LORD again. He also did a lot of work on the wall at the hill of Ophel.
4 He built cities in the hill country of Judah. He also built strong forts and watchtowers in the forests.

5 Jotham went to war with the king of the Ammonites and won. That year they gave him a hundred talents of silver. They also gave him ten thousand cors of wheat and ten thousand cors of barley. They paid him the same in the second and third years.
6 So Jotham became strong, because he lived his life before the LORD his God.
7 The other things Jotham did, with all his wars and his ways, are written in the Book of the Kings of Israel and Judah.
8 He was twenty-five years old when he became king. He was king in Jerusalem for sixteen years.
9 Then Jotham died like his fathers. He was buried in the City of David. And his son Ahaz became king after him.

title: 2 Chronicles 28
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 Ahaz was twenty years old when he became king. He ruled in Jerusalem for sixteen years. He did not do what was right in the LORD's eyes, as his father David had done.
2 Instead, he walked in the ways of the kings of Israel. He even made metal idols of Baal.
3 Ahaz also burned incense in the Valley of Hinnom. He even sacrificed his sons in the fire. He copied the terrible practices of the nations that the LORD had driven out before Israel.
4 He sacrificed and burned incense at the high places. He did this on the hills and under every green tree.
5 So the LORD his God handed Ahaz over to the king of Aram. The king attacked him and took many captives to Damascus. Ahaz was also given into the hand of the king of Israel, who struck him hard.
6 In one day, Pekah son of Remaliah killed 120,000 brave soldiers in Judah. This happened because they had left the LORD, the God of their fathers.
7 Zichri, a strong man of Ephraim, killed Maaseiah the king's son. He also killed Azrikam, the head of the palace, and Elkanah, second to the king.
8 Then the men of Israel took 200,000 captives from their fellow Israelites. They took women, sons, and daughters. They also carried off many stolen goods to Samaria.

9 But a prophet of the LORD named Oded was there. He went out to meet the army returning to Samaria. He said to them, "Look, the LORD, the God of your fathers, was angry with Judah. So He handed them over to you. But you have killed them in a rage that reaches up to heaven.
10 And now you plan to make the men and women of Judah and Jerusalem your slaves. But are you not also guilty before the LORD your God?
11 Now listen to me. Send back the captives you took from your fellow Israelites. The fierce anger of the LORD is upon you."
12 Then some leaders of Ephraim stood against the soldiers coming from the war. They were Azariah son of Jehohanan, Berechiah son of Meshillemoth, Jehizkiah son of Shallum, and Amasa son of Hadlai.
13 They said, "You must not bring the captives here. You will bring guilt upon us from the LORD. You will add to our sins and our guilt. Our guilt is already great, and fierce anger is upon Israel."
14 So the soldiers left the captives and the stolen goods before the leaders and all the assembly.

15 Then the men chosen by name stood up and took care of the captives. They used the stolen goods to clothe those who had no clothes. They gave them sandals, food, and drink. They put oil on their wounds. They put the weak ones on donkeys. So they brought them to Jericho, the City of Palms, to their brothers. Then they returned to Samaria.
16 At that time, King Ahaz sent for help from the king of Assyria.
17 The Edomites had come again and attacked Judah. They had carried away captives.
18 The Philistines had also raided the cities of the foothills and the Negev of Judah. They captured Beth-shemesh, Aijalon, Gederoth, Soco, Timnah, and Gimzo, with their villages.
19 For the LORD made Judah low because Ahaz king of Israel acted without control in Judah. He had been very unfaithful to the LORD.
20 Then Tiglath-pileser, king of Assyria, came to Ahaz. But he troubled him instead of making him strong.
21 Ahaz took some treasures from the house of the LORD, from the royal palace, and from the princes. He gave it all to the king of Assyria. But it did not help him.
22 In his time of trouble, King Ahaz became even more unfaithful to the LORD.

23 Since Damascus had defeated him, he sacrificed to their gods. He said, "The gods of the kings of Aram helped them. So I will sacrifice to them, and they will help me." But these gods caused the ruin of Ahaz and all Israel.
24 Then Ahaz gathered the items from the house of God and cut them into pieces. He shut the doors of the house of the LORD. He set up his own altars on every street corner in Jerusalem.
25 In every city of Judah, he built high places to burn incense to other gods. So he made the LORD, the God of his fathers, angry.
26 The rest of the acts of Ahaz, from beginning to end, are written in the Book of the Kings of Judah and Israel.
27 Ahaz died and was buried in the city of Jerusalem. But he was not placed in the tombs of the kings of Israel. His son Hezekiah ruled in his place.

title: 2 Chronicles 29
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 Hezekiah was twenty-five years old when he became king. He ruled in Jerusalem for twenty-nine years. His mother was Abijah, the daughter of Zechariah.
2 He did what was right in the eyes of the LORD, just as his father David had done.
3 In the first month of his first year as king, Hezekiah opened the doors of the house of the LORD and repaired them.
4 Then he brought in the priests and Levites. He gathered them in the open square on the east side.
5 He said, "Listen to me, O Levites. Consecrate yourselves now, and consecrate the house of the LORD, the God of your fathers. Take every unclean thing out of the Holy Place.
6 Our fathers were not faithful. They did evil in the sight of the LORD our God. They left Him. They turned their faces away from the place where the LORD lives, and they turned their backs on Him.
7 They also shut the doors of the front porch and put out the lamps. They did not burn incense or bring burnt offerings in the Holy Place of the God of Israel.
8 So the strong anger of the LORD has come on Judah and Jerusalem. He has made them something people fear, hate, and laugh at, as you can see with your own eyes.
9 This is why our fathers have fallen by the sword. Our sons, daughters, and wives have been taken away as prisoners.
10 Now I want in my heart to make a covenant with the LORD, the God of Israel. Then His strong anger will turn away from us.
11 My sons, do not be lazy. The LORD has chosen you to stand before Him, to serve Him, to minister in His presence, and to burn incense."

12 Then the Levites set to work: Mahath son of Amasai and Joel son of Azariah from the Kohathites; Kish son of Abdi and Azariah son of Jehallelel from the Merarites; Joah son of Zimmah and Eden son of Joah from the Gershonites;
13 Shimri and Jeuel from the Elizaphanites; Zechariah and Mattaniah from the Asaphites;
14 Jehiel and Shimei from the Hemanites; and Shemaiah and Uzziel from the Jeduthunites.
15 They gathered their brothers and consecrated themselves. Then they went in to cleanse the house of the LORD, as the king had commanded by the words of the LORD.

16 The priests went inside the house of the LORD to cleanse it. They brought out to the courtyard every unclean thing they found in the temple of the LORD. Then the Levites took these things to the Kidron Valley.

17 They began the consecration on the first day of the first month. On the eighth day of the month they reached the front porch of the LORD. For eight more days they consecrated the house of the LORD itself. They finished on the sixteenth day of the first month.

18 Then they went in to King Hezekiah and said, "We have cleansed all the house of the LORD. We cleansed the altar of burnt offering with all its tools, and the table of the showbread with all its tools.
19 Also, we have prepared and consecrated all the things that King Ahaz threw away when he turned from God during his rule. They are now in front of the altar of the LORD."
20 Early the next morning King Hezekiah gathered the city leaders and went up to the house of the LORD.

21 They brought seven bulls, seven rams, seven lambs, and seven male goats. These were a sin offering for the kingdom, for the holy temple, and for Judah. The king commanded the priests, the sons of Aaron, to offer them on the altar of the LORD.

22 So they killed the bulls, and the priests took the blood and sprinkled it on the altar. They killed the rams and sprinkled the blood on the altar. They killed the lambs and sprinkled the blood on the altar.
23 Then they brought the goats for the sin offering before the king and the people. The people laid their hands on them.

24 The priests killed the goats and put their blood on the altar as a sin offering. This was to make atonement to cover the sin of all Israel. The king had ordered the burnt offering and the sin offering for all Israel.

25 Hezekiah placed the Levites in the house of the LORD with cymbals, harps, and lyres. This was the command of David, of Gad the king's seer, and of Nathan the prophet. The command had come from the LORD through His prophets.
26 So the Levites stood with the instruments of David, and the priests stood with the trumpets.
27 Hezekiah ordered that the burnt offering be made on the altar. When the burnt offering began, the song of the LORD and the trumpets began too, with the instruments of David king of Israel.
28 All the people were worshiping. The singers were singing, and the trumpeters were playing. All this went on until the burnt offering was finished.
29 When the offerings were finished, the king and all the people there bowed down and worshiped.
30 Then King Hezekiah and his leaders told the Levites to sing praises to the LORD with the words of David and of Asaph the seer. So they sang praises with joy, bowed their heads, and worshiped.

31 Then Hezekiah said, "Now you have consecrated yourselves to the LORD. Come near and bring sacrifices and thank offerings to the house of the LORD." So the people brought sacrifices and thank offerings, and all who were willing in heart brought burnt offerings.
32 The number of burnt offerings the people brought was seventy bulls, a hundred rams, and two hundred lambs. All these were a burnt offering to the LORD.
33 The consecrated offerings were six hundred bulls and three thousand sheep.

34 But there were not enough priests to skin all the burnt offerings. So their Levite brothers helped them until the work was finished and until more priests had been consecrated. The Levites had been more careful and ready to consecrate themselves than the priests.
35 There were very many burnt offerings, together with the fat of the peace offerings and the drink offerings for the burnt offerings. So the service of the house of the LORD was set in order again.
36 Then Hezekiah and all the people were glad for what God had prepared for the people, because everything had been done so quickly.

title: 2 Chronicles 30
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 Then Hezekiah sent a message through all Israel and Judah. He also wrote letters to Ephraim and Manasseh. He asked them to come to the house of the LORD in Jerusalem. They were to keep the Passover of the LORD, the God of Israel.
2 The king, his officials, and the whole group in Jerusalem had decided to keep the Passover in the second month.
3 They could not keep it at the normal time. Not enough priests had made themselves ready, and the people had not yet come to Jerusalem.
4 The plan pleased the king and the whole group.

5 So they made a decree to send word through all Israel, from Beersheba to Dan. The people should come to Jerusalem to keep the Passover of the LORD, the God of Israel. They had not kept it as one nation in the right way for a long time.

6 So the messengers went through Israel and Judah with letters from the king and his officials. The letters said: “Children of Israel, return to the LORD, the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Israel. Then He will return to those of you who are left, who have escaped the hand of the kings of Assyria.
7 Do not be like your fathers and brothers. They were not loyal to the LORD, the God of their fathers. So He made them a thing that shocks people, as you can see.

8 Now do not be stubborn as your fathers were. Give yourselves to the LORD and come to His holy place. He has set it apart for ever. Serve the LORD your God, so His strong anger will turn away from you.

9 If you return to the LORD, your brothers and sons will be shown mercy by those who took them. They will return to this land. The LORD your God is kind and full of mercy. He will not turn His face from you if you return to Him.”
10 The messengers went from city to city through Ephraim and Manasseh, even to Zebulun. But the people laughed at them and made fun of them.
11 Still, some men from Asher, Manasseh, and Zebulun made themselves humble and came to Jerusalem.
12 Also, the power of God was on the people of Judah. He gave them one heart to obey the king and his officials, as the word of the LORD said.
13 In the second month a very large group met in Jerusalem to keep the Feast of Bread Without Yeast.
14 They began to remove the altars in Jerusalem. They took away the altars for burning incense and threw them into the Kidron Valley.
15 On the fourteenth day of the second month they killed the Passover lamb. The priests and Levites felt ashamed. They made themselves ready and brought burnt offerings to the house of the LORD.
16 They stood in the places set for them, as the Law of Moses, the man of God, said. The priests put the blood on the altar, blood they took from the Levites.
17 Many people in the group had not made themselves ready. So the Levites killed the Passover lambs for every person who was not clean. They set the lambs apart for the LORD.

18 A large number of people had not made themselves pure. Many were from Ephraim, Manasseh, Issachar, and Zebulun. They ate the Passover, but not in the way that was written. But Hezekiah prayed for them. He said, “May the LORD, who is good, provide atonement for everyone
19 who sets his heart on seeking God—the LORD, the God of his fathers—even if he is not made clean by the rules of the holy place.”
20 The LORD heard Hezekiah and healed the people.
21 The Israelites in Jerusalem kept the Feast of Bread Without Yeast for seven days with great joy. The Levites and priests praised the LORD every day with loud instruments of praise.
22 Hezekiah spoke kind words to all the Levites who served the LORD with skill. For seven days they ate their share, offered peace offerings, and gave thanks to the LORD, the God of their fathers.
23 The whole group agreed to keep the feast for seven more days. So they kept seven more days with joy.
24 Hezekiah king of Judah gave the group a thousand bulls and seven thousand sheep. The officials gave the group a thousand bulls and ten thousand sheep. And many priests made themselves ready.
25 The whole group from Judah was glad, with the priests and Levites. The whole group from Israel was glad too, with the foreigners who came from Israel and those who lived in Judah.
26 There was great joy in Jerusalem. Nothing like this had happened there since the days of Solomon son of David, king of Israel.
27 Then the priests and the Levites stood up to bless the people. God heard their voice, and their prayer came to His holy home in heaven.

title: 2 Chronicles 31
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 When all this was finished, the Israelites who were there went out to the towns of Judah. They broke the sacred stones. They cut down the Asherah poles. They tore down the high places of worship and altars in all Judah and Benjamin, and also in Ephraim and Manasseh. They destroyed them all. Then all the Israelites went back to their towns, each to his own land.

2 Hezekiah set up again the groups of the priests and Levites. Each one had his own job as a priest or Levite. They did the burnt offerings and peace offerings. They served, gave thanks, and sang praise at the gates of the LORD’s house.
3 The king gave from his own things for the regular morning and evening burnt offerings. He also gave for the burnt offerings on the Sabbaths, New Moons, and set feasts, as the Law of the LORD says.
4 He also told the people in Jerusalem to give a share for the priests and Levites. Then they could give all their time to the Law of the LORD.

5 As soon as the order went out, the Israelites gave freely. They brought the firstfruits of grain, new wine, oil, and honey. They brought from all the crops of the field. They brought a lot — a tithe of everything.

6 The Israelites and Judahites who lived in the towns of Judah also brought a tithe of their cattle and sheep. They brought a tithe of the holy things set apart for the LORD their God. They laid them in big piles.
7 In the third month they began making the piles. They finished in the seventh month.
8 When Hezekiah and his officials came and saw the piles, they blessed the LORD and His people Israel.
9 Then Hezekiah asked the priests and Levites about the piles.

10 Azariah, the chief priest from the family of Zadok, answered him. He said, “Since the people began to bring their gifts into the house of the LORD, we have had enough to eat, and much is left over. The LORD has blessed His people. This great pile is what is left over.”
11 Then Hezekiah told them to get storage rooms ready in the house of the LORD, and they did so.
12 They brought in the gifts, tithes, and holy gifts faithfully. Conaniah the Levite was in charge of them. His brother Shimei was second.

13 Jehiel, Azaziah, Nahath, Asahel, Jerimoth, Jozabad, Eliel, Ismachiah, Mahath, and Benaiah were helpers. They worked under Conaniah and his brother Shimei. King Hezekiah and Azariah, the chief officer of the house of God, chose them.
14 Kore son of Imnah the Levite was the keeper of the East Gate. He was in charge of the freewill offerings given to God. He gave out the gifts to the LORD and the holy gifts.
15 Under him were Eden, Miniamin, Jeshua, Shemaiah, Amariah, and Shecaniah. They faithfully gave shares to their fellow priests in their towns. They gave to the divisions, old and young alike.
16 They also gave shares to the males in the family list who were three years old or more. These were all who would enter the house of the LORD for their daily work in the duties of their groups.
17 They gave to the priests listed by family in the family list. They also gave to the Levites twenty years old or more, by their jobs and groups.
18 The family list included all the little ones, wives, sons, and daughters in the whole group. For they had faithfully set themselves apart as holy.

19 As for the priests, the sons of Aaron, who lived on the farmland near their towns or in other towns — men were chosen by name to give a share to every male priest and to every Levite on the family list.
20 This is what Hezekiah did in all Judah. He did what was good and right and true before the LORD his God.
21 He worked hard in every work he began for the house of God, the law, and the commands, in order to seek his God. And so he did well.

title: 2 Chronicles 32
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 After these faithful acts, Sennacherib king of Assyria came and attacked Judah. He surrounded the strong walled cities and planned to take them.
2 When Hezekiah saw that Sennacherib had come to fight against Jerusalem,
3 he talked with his leaders and commanders about blocking the springs outside the city. They helped him do it.
4 Many people came together and blocked all the springs and the stream that ran through the land. They said, "Why should the kings of Assyria come and find plenty of water?"

5 Then Hezekiah worked with great effort to rebuild every broken part of the wall. He built towers on it. He also built an outer wall and made the support walls of the City of David stronger. He made many weapons and shields.
6 Hezekiah chose military commanders over the people. He gathered them in the open square at the city gate. He encouraged them, saying,
7 "Be strong and brave! Do not be afraid or worried because of the king of Assyria and his huge army. The One with us is greater than the one with him.
8 With him is only human strength, but with us is the LORD our God. He will help us and fight our battles." So the people were made strong by the words of Hezekiah king of Judah.

9 Later, while Sennacherib king of Assyria and all his army surrounded Lachish, he sent his servants to Jerusalem. They brought a message for King Hezekiah of Judah and all the people of Judah in Jerusalem:
10 "This is what Sennacherib king of Assyria says: Why do you trust so much? Why do you stay in Jerusalem while it is surrounded?
11 Is Hezekiah not tricking you? He wants you to die from hunger and thirst. He says, 'The LORD our God will save us from the king of Assyria.'
12 Did Hezekiah himself not remove the LORD's high places and altars? He told Judah and Jerusalem, 'You must worship before one altar, and burn sacrifices on it.'
13 Do you not know what I and my fathers have done to all the peoples of other lands? Could the gods of those nations ever save their land from my hand?
14 Of all the gods that my fathers fully destroyed, which one could save his people from my hand? How then can your God save you from me?
15 So now, do not let Hezekiah trick you or fool you like this. Do not believe him. No god of any nation or kingdom could save his people from me or from my fathers. Your God can save you even less!"
16 And the servants of Sennacherib spoke more against the LORD God and against His servant Hezekiah.

17 He also wrote letters making fun of the LORD, the God of Israel. He said, "Just as the gods of the nations did not save their people from my hand, so the God of Hezekiah will not save His people from me."
18 Then the Assyrians shouted loudly in Hebrew to the people of Jerusalem on the wall. They wanted to scare them and make them afraid so they could take the city.
19 They spoke against the God of Jerusalem as if He were like the gods of the peoples of the earth—gods made by human hands.
20 Then King Hezekiah and the prophet Isaiah son of Amoz cried out to heaven in prayer.

21 And the LORD sent an angel who killed all the strong soldiers and every leader and commander in the camp of the king of Assyria. So he went back to his own land with shame. When he entered the temple of his god, some of his own sons killed him with the sword there.
22 So the LORD saved Hezekiah and the people of Jerusalem from the hand of King Sennacherib of Assyria and from all others. He gave them rest on every side.
23 Many people brought offerings to Jerusalem for the LORD and valuable gifts for Hezekiah king of Judah. From that time on he was lifted high in the eyes of all nations.
24 In those days Hezekiah became very sick, near death. So he prayed to the LORD. The LORD spoke to him and gave him a sign.
25 But because his heart was proud, Hezekiah did not give thanks for the kindness shown to him. So anger came upon him and upon Judah and Jerusalem.
26 Then Hezekiah turned from the pride of his heart, and the people of Jerusalem did too. So the anger of the LORD did not come upon them during the days of Hezekiah.
27 Hezekiah had very great riches and honor. He made storerooms for his silver, gold, precious stones, spices, shields, and all kinds of valuable things.
28 He also made storehouses for the harvest of grain, new wine, and oil. He built stalls for all kinds of cattle and pens for the flocks.
29 He made cities for himself. He gained many sheep and cattle, for God gave him very great wealth.
30 It was Hezekiah who blocked the upper outlet of the Spring of Gihon. He sent the water down to the west side of the City of David. Hezekiah did well in everything he did.
31 When messengers from the rulers of Babylon came to ask about the wonder that had happened in the land, God left him alone to test him. He wanted to know all that was in Hezekiah's heart.
32 As for the rest of the acts of Hezekiah and his deeds of faithful love, they are written in the vision of the prophet Isaiah son of Amoz, in the Book of the Kings of Judah and Israel.

33 And Hezekiah died and joined his fathers. He was buried in the upper tombs of David's descendants. All Judah and the people of Jerusalem honored him at his death. And his son Manasseh became king in his place.

title: 2 Chronicles 33
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 Manasseh was twelve years old when he became king. He ruled in Jerusalem for fifty-five years.
2 He did evil in the sight of the LORD. He followed the hated practices of the nations that the LORD had driven out before Israel.
3 He rebuilt the high places that his father Hezekiah had torn down. He set up altars for the Baals and made Asherah poles. He worshiped and served all the stars and powers of the sky.
4 Manasseh also built altars in the house of the LORD. About this house the LORD had said, "My Name will stay in Jerusalem forever."
5 In both courtyards of the house of the LORD, he built altars to all the stars and powers of the sky.

6 He burned his own sons in the fire in the Valley of Hinnom. He used magic, fortune-telling, and witchcraft. He asked help from people who talk to spirits and the dead. He did great evil in the sight of the LORD and made Him angry.

7 Manasseh even took the carved idol he had made and put it in the house of God. About this house God had said to David and his son Solomon, "In this temple and in Jerusalem, which I chose from all the tribes of Israel, I will set My Name forever.
8 I will never again make the feet of Israel leave the land I gave to your fathers. But they must carefully obey all that I commanded them through Moses—all the laws, rules, and decisions."
9 So Manasseh led Judah and Jerusalem the wrong way. They did worse evil than the nations the LORD had destroyed before Israel.
10 The LORD spoke to Manasseh and his people, but they did not listen.
11 So the LORD brought the army commanders of the king of Assyria against them. They captured Manasseh. They put a hook in his nose and bound him with bronze chains. They took him to Babylon.
12 In his trouble, Manasseh asked for help from the LORD his God. He humbled himself deeply before the God of his fathers.
13 He prayed to Him, and the LORD listened to him and heard his prayer. So God brought him back to Jerusalem and to his kingdom. Then Manasseh knew that the LORD is God.

14 After this, Manasseh rebuilt the outer wall of the City of David. It went from west of Gihon in the valley to the entrance of the Fish Gate. It went around the hill of Ophel, and he made it much higher. He also placed army commanders in all the strong cities of Judah.
15 He removed the foreign gods and the idol from the house of the LORD. He took away all the altars he had built on the temple mount and in Jerusalem. He threw them outside the city.
16 Then he repaired the altar of the LORD. He offered peace offerings and thank offerings on it. He told Judah to serve the LORD, the God of Israel.
17 Yet the people still offered sacrifices at the high places. But they offered them only to the LORD their God.

18 The rest of the things Manasseh did, his prayer to his God, and the words of the seers who spoke to him in the name of the LORD, the God of Israel, are written in the Chronicles of the Kings of Israel.

19 His prayer and how God heard him, all his sin and his unfaithful acts, and the places where he built high places and set up Asherah poles and idols before he humbled himself, are written in the Records of the Seers.
20 Manasseh rested with his fathers and was buried at his palace. His son Amon became king in his place.
21 Amon was twenty-two years old when he became king. He ruled in Jerusalem two years.
22 He did evil in the sight of the LORD, like his father Manasseh had done. Amon served and offered sacrifices to all the idols his father Manasseh had made.
23 But he did not humble himself before the LORD as his father Manasseh had done. Instead, Amon made his guilt greater.
24 Then Amon's servants made a secret plan against him and killed him in his palace.
25 But the people of the land killed all those who had killed King Amon. They made his son Josiah king in his place.

title: 2 Chronicles 34
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 Josiah was eight years old when he became king. He ruled in Jerusalem for thirty-one years.
2 He did what was right before the LORD. He walked in the ways of his father David. He did not turn away to the right or to the left.

3 In the eighth year of his rule, while he was still young, Josiah began to seek the God of his father David. In the twelfth year he began to clean Judah and Jerusalem. He removed the high places, the Asherah poles, the carved idols, and the metal images.

4 Workers tore down the altars of the Baals in front of him. He cut into pieces the incense altars that stood above them. He broke the Asherah poles, the carved idols, and the metal images. He crushed them to dust. He scattered the dust on the graves of those who had offered to them.
5 Then he burned the bones of their priests on their altars. So he cleaned Judah and Jerusalem.
6 Josiah did the same in the cities of Manasseh, Ephraim, and Simeon, as far as Naphtali, and in the broken places around them.
7 He tore down the altars and the Asherah poles. He crushed the idols to powder. He cut into pieces all the incense altars in the land of Israel. Then he returned to Jerusalem.

8 In the eighteenth year of his rule, Josiah wanted to clean the land and the temple. So he sent Shaphan son of Azaliah, Maaseiah the city governor, and Joah son of Joahaz, the record keeper. They were to repair the house of the LORD his God.

9 They went to Hilkiah the high priest. They gave him the money that had been brought into the house of God. The Levites at the doors had collected it from the people of Manasseh and Ephraim. They also took it from all the rest of Israel, Judah, Benjamin, and the people of Jerusalem.
10 They gave the money to the leaders over the work of the house of the LORD. These leaders paid the workmen who were fixing and repairing the house of the LORD.
11 They also gave money to the carpenters and builders. The money paid for cut stone and wood for joining and beams. The kings of Judah had let these buildings fall apart.

12 The men did the work faithfully. The Levites who watched over them were Jahath and Obadiah, from the family of Merari. Zechariah and Meshullam, from the family of Kohath, helped too. Other Levites, skilled with music,
13 were over the workers. They watched all who did each task. Some Levites were secretaries, officers, and gatekeepers.
14 While they were bringing out the money that had been taken into the house of the LORD, Hilkiah the priest found the Book of the Law of the LORD. Moses had written it.
15 Hilkiah said to Shaphan the scribe, "I have found the Book of the Law in the house of the LORD!" Then he gave it to Shaphan.
16 Shaphan brought the book to the king. He reported, "Your servants are doing all that you gave them to do.
17 They have paid out the money found in the house of the LORD. They have put it into the hands of the leaders and workers."
18 Shaphan the scribe also told the king, "Hilkiah the priest has given me a book." Then Shaphan read it before the king.
19 When the king heard the words of the Law, he tore his clothes.
20 He gave a command to Hilkiah, Ahikam son of Shaphan, Abdon son of Micah, Shaphan the scribe, and Asaiah the servant of the king:

21 "Go and ask the LORD for me. Ask for those who remain in Israel and Judah. Ask about the words in the book that has been found. Great is the anger of the LORD against us. Our fathers did not keep the word of the LORD or do all that is written in this book."

22 So Hilkiah and the men the king sent went to Huldah the prophetess. She was the wife of Shallum son of Tokhath, the son of Hasrah, the keeper of the wardrobe. She lived in Jerusalem, in the Second District.
23 Huldah said to them, "This is what the LORD, the God of Israel, says: 'Tell the man who sent you
24 that this is what the LORD says: I am about to bring trouble on this place and on its people. All the curses written in the book read before the king of Judah will come.
25 They have left Me and burned incense to other gods. They have made Me angry with all the work of their hands. My anger will be poured out on this place and will not be stopped.'
26 But to the king of Judah, who sent you to ask the LORD, say this: 'This is what the LORD, the God of Israel, says about the words you heard:

27 Your heart was soft and you humbled yourself before God when you heard His words against this place and its people. You humbled yourself before Me. You tore your clothes and wept before Me. So I have heard you,' says the LORD.

28 'Now I will let you join your fathers in death. You will be buried in your grave in peace. Your eyes will not see all the trouble that I will bring on this place and its people.'" So they brought her answer back to the king.
29 Then the king called together all the elders of Judah and Jerusalem.

30 He went up to the house of the LORD with all the people of Judah and Jerusalem. The priests and the Levites went too. All the people, small and great, were there. He read to them all the words of the Book of the Covenant found in the house of the LORD.

31 The king stood by the pillar and made a covenant before the LORD. He promised to follow the LORD and to keep His commandments, rules, and laws with all his heart and all his soul. He would carry out the words of the covenant written in this book.
32 Then he had everyone in Jerusalem and Benjamin agree to the covenant. So all the people of Jerusalem carried out the covenant of God, the God of their fathers.

33 Josiah removed all the hated and evil things from all the lands of the Israelites. He made everyone in Israel serve the LORD their God. As long as he ruled, they did not turn away from following the LORD, the God of their fathers.

title: 2 Chronicles 35
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 Then Josiah held the Passover to the LORD in Jerusalem. They killed the Passover lamb on the fourteenth day of the first month.
2 He gave the priests their jobs. He helped them serve in the house of the LORD.

3 Josiah said to the Levites who taught all Israel and were holy to the LORD: "Put the holy ark in the temple that Solomon son of David king of Israel built. You do not need to carry it on your shoulders anymore. Now serve the LORD your God and His people Israel.
4 Get ready by your families and groups. Follow the rules written by David king of Israel and his son Solomon.
5 Stand in the Holy Place by the family groups of your fellow people who are not priests, and by the groups of the Levite families.
6 Kill the Passover lambs. Make yourselves holy. Get things ready for your fellow countrymen. Do what the LORD said through Moses."
7 Josiah gave from his own flocks and herds. He gave 30,000 lambs and goats and 3,000 bulls for the Passover offerings for all the people there.
8 His officials also gave gladly to the people, priests, and Levites. Hilkiah, Zechariah, and Jehiel, the leaders of God's house, gave the priests 2,600 Passover offerings and 300 bulls.
9 Also, Conaniah and his brothers Shemaiah and Nethanel, with Hashabiah, Jeiel, and Jozabad, leaders of the Levites, gave the Levites 5,000 Passover offerings and 500 bulls.
10 So the service was ready. The priests stood in their places, and the Levites in their groups, just as the king had ordered.
11 They killed the Passover lambs. The priests put the blood on the altar, and the Levites took the skin off the animals.
12 They set aside the burnt offerings. These were given to the family groups of the people, so they could offer them to the LORD, as is written in the Book of Moses. They did the same with the bulls.
13 They roasted the Passover animals on the fire, following the rule. They boiled the other holy offerings in pots, kettles, and bowls. They quickly brought them to all the people.

14 After that, they got food ready for themselves and for the priests. The priests, the sons of Aaron, were busy offering burnt offerings and fat until night. So the Levites got food ready for themselves and for the priests, the sons of Aaron.

15 The singers, the sons of Asaph, stood at their places. This followed the order of David, Asaph, Heman, and Jeduthun the king's prophet. The gatekeepers at each gate did not need to leave their places. Their fellow Levites got things ready for them.
16 So on that day all the service of the LORD was done. They held the Passover and offered burnt offerings on the altar of the LORD, just as King Josiah had ordered.
17 The Israelites who were there held the Passover at that time. They also kept the Feast of Bread Without Yeast for seven days.

18 No Passover like this had been held in Israel since the days of Samuel the prophet. No king of Israel had ever held a Passover like the one Josiah held. He held it with the priests, the Levites, all Judah, the Israelites who were there, and the people of Jerusalem.
19 This Passover was held in the eighteenth year of Josiah's reign.
20 After all this, when Josiah had set the temple in order, Neco king of Egypt marched up to fight at Carchemish by the Euphrates. Josiah went out to face him.

21 But Neco sent messengers to him, saying, "What is the problem between you and me, O king of Judah? I have not come to fight you today. I am fighting another royal family. God told me to hurry. So stop fighting against God, who is with me, or He will destroy you!"
22 But Josiah did not turn away. He dressed so people would not know him, so he could fight Neco. He did not listen to Neco's words from God's mouth. He went to fight him on the Plain of Megiddo.
23 There the archers shot King Josiah. He said to his servants, "Take me away, for I am badly hurt!"

24 So his servants took him out of his chariot. They put him in his second chariot and brought him to Jerusalem. There he died. Josiah was buried in the tomb of his fathers. All Judah and Jerusalem cried for him.
25 Then Jeremiah sang sad songs for Josiah. To this day all the singers, men and women, sing sad songs for Josiah. They made these songs a rule for Israel. They are written in the Book of Sad Songs.
26 The rest of the acts of Josiah, with his faithful love, follow what is written in the Law of the LORD.
27 His acts from start to end are written in the Book of the Kings of Israel and Judah.

title: 2 Chronicles 36
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 2 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 Then the people of the land took Jehoahaz son of Josiah. They made him king in Jerusalem after his father.
2 Jehoahaz was twenty-three years old when he became king. He ruled in Jerusalem for three months.
3 The king of Egypt removed him from being king in Jerusalem. He made Judah pay a tax of a hundred talents of silver and a talent of gold.
4 Then Neco king of Egypt made Eliakim, the brother of Jehoahaz, king over Judah and Jerusalem. He changed Eliakim’s name to Jehoiakim. But Neco took Jehoahaz away to Egypt.
5 Jehoiakim was twenty-five years old when he became king. He ruled in Jerusalem eleven years. He did evil in the sight of the LORD his God.
6 Then Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon came up against Jehoiakim. He tied him with bronze chains to take him to Babylon.
7 Nebuchadnezzar also took some items from the house of the LORD to Babylon. He put them in his own temple in Babylon.
8 The other acts of Jehoiakim, the terrible sinful acts he did, and all that was found against him, are written in the Book of the Kings of Israel and Judah. His son Jehoiachin became king after him.
9 Jehoiachin was eighteen years old when he became king. He ruled in Jerusalem three months and ten days. He did evil in the sight of the LORD.

10 In the spring, King Nebuchadnezzar called for Jehoiachin. He brought him to Babylon with the valuable items from the house of the LORD. He made Jehoiachin’s relative Zedekiah king over Judah and Jerusalem.
11 Zedekiah was twenty-one years old when he became king. He ruled in Jerusalem eleven years.
12 He did evil in the sight of the LORD his God. He did not humble himself before Jeremiah the prophet, who spoke for the LORD.
13 He also turned against King Nebuchadnezzar, who had made him swear by God. Zedekiah refused to listen. He made his heart hard and would not turn to the LORD, the God of Israel.

14 All the leaders of the priests and the people sinned more and more. They followed all the terrible acts of the nations. They made the house of the LORD unclean, the house He had set apart as holy in Jerusalem.
15 Again and again the LORD, the God of their fathers, sent word to His people through His messengers. He felt sorry for them and for His dwelling place.
16 But they made fun of God’s messengers. They hated His words and laughed at His prophets. So the anger of the LORD against His people grew so great it could not be stopped.

17 So He brought the king of the Chaldeans against them. He killed their young men with the sword inside the sanctuary. He did not spare young men or young women, old people or sick people. God gave them all into the hand of Nebuchadnezzar.
18 Nebuchadnezzar carried everything off to Babylon. He took all the items of the house of God, big and small. He took the treasures of the house of the LORD and of the king and his officials.
19 Then the Chaldeans set fire to the house of God. They broke down the wall of Jerusalem. They burned all the palaces and destroyed every valuable item.
20 Those who escaped the sword were carried by Nebuchadnezzar into exile in Babylon. They became servants to him and his sons until the kingdom of Persia came to power.
21 So the land enjoyed its Sabbath rest all the days it lay empty. This lasted until seventy years were complete, just as the LORD had said through Jeremiah.

22 In the first year of Cyrus king of Persia, the LORD made the words of Jeremiah come true. The LORD moved the heart of Cyrus king of Persia to send a message through all his kingdom and to put it in writing:

23 “This is what Cyrus king of Persia says: ‘The LORD, the God of heaven, has given me all the kingdoms of the earth. He has chosen me to build a house for Him at Jerusalem in Judah. Whoever among you belongs to His people, may the LORD his God be with him, and may he go up.’”
